description:
auto-update-branches.yml uses default GITHUB_TOKEN to call `gh api PUT /pulls/{N}/update-branch`. GitHub's recursion rule: pushes authored by GITHUB_TOKEN do NOT fire downstream workflows. Result: every auto-updated PR head ends up with zero CI check-runs, and master's strict required_status_checks leaves the PR permanently BLOCKED+MERGEABLE.

Same class as li-8f3 (auto-enable-merge needed App token because GITHUB_TOKEN couldn't enable auto-merge). Same fix: mint a GH App installation token via actions/create-github-app-token@v1 and use that token for the update-branch call. The header comment in auto-update-branches.yml currently argues for keeping GITHUB_TOKEN per least-privilege, but overlooks that the workflow-recursion ceiling applies to its push, not just to auto-merge mutations.

Observed 2026-05-11: PRs #59 #60 #63 #64 #65 #86 all stranded BLOCKED+MERGEABLE with 0 check-runs on head SHA. Unstuck by close+reopen from PAT (triggers CI). PRs #61 #62 were also stranded but superseded — closed.

Secondary bug (file separately if desired): ci.yml detect-py-changes uses `git diff origin/master...HEAD` but actions/checkout on pull_request events doesn't reliably create origin/master remote-tracking ref; 3/6 reruns hit `fatal: origin/master...HEAD: no merge base`. Fix: explicit `git fetch origin master` (full depth — fetch-depth: 0 is already set in checkout) before the diff.
title: auto-update-branches uses GITHUB_TOKEN — pushes don't trigger CI, stranding PRs

description: One-shot migration script living inside livespec-impl-plaintext (callable via 'just <recipe>'). description: The commit-msg hook check at dev-tooling/checks/red_green_replay.py main() has three classifiable fallthrough cases at line 140 that all silently return 1 with no structlog.error: (1) staged paths classify as neither tests nor impl (the case observed when committing config/template files under feat: — e.g., templates/impl-plugin/copier.yml + .beads/issues.jsonl); (2) tests and impl are both staged without prior Red trailers (mixed-buckets); (3) impl is staged but HEAD has no Red trailers (Green attempted without preceding Red). The user-facing symptom is 'lefthook rejected the commit but I have no idea why'. Existing diagnostics fire for empty-staged (line 126) and inside the mode handlers, but the line 140 fallthrough is silent. Fix: distinguish the three cases and emit a structured diagnostic (check_id + hint) for each — particularly the 'staged paths are neither tests nor impl' case should hint the user toward the exempt commit types (chore/docs/build/ci/style/test/refactor/perf/revert) for non-code changes. Reproduce: commit a non-tests/non-impl staged file (e.g., templates/foo.yml) with a feat: subject. Observed 2026-05-19 while landing Phase C.1 PR #116 (workaround was to switch to chore(template): which is exempt).
reason: Fix for silent fallthrough landed via PR #118 (Red→Green amended commit). Added _diagnose_fallthrough helper emitting three distinct structlog.error diagnostics with stable check_ids: red-green-replay-staged-not-classifiable, red-green-replay-mixed-buckets, red-green-replay-green-without-red. Three new tests in tests/dev-tooling/checks/test_red_green_replay.py cover each fallthrough case.
title: [bug] red_green_replay.py main() line 140 fallthrough exits non-zero with no diagnostic

description:
ci.yml's `detect-py-changes` step runs:

    git fetch origin "$BASE_REF" --depth=1 2>/dev/null || true
    changeset=$(git diff --name-only "origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD")

On `pull_request` events, actions/checkout@v4 does not reliably create the `origin/master` remote-tracking ref even with `fetch-depth: 0` — the shallow `--depth=1` fetch can land without a common ancestor reachable, and the diff fails with:

    fatal: origin/master...HEAD: no merge base
    ##[error]Process completed with exit code 128.

Observed 2026-05-11: 3 of 6 close+reopen-triggered CI runs (#64, #65, #86) failed here on first attempt; rerunning the same run with no code change passed. Pure flake.

Fix: drop the `--depth=1` (fetch-depth: 0 already pulls full history in the checkout) and remove the silent `|| true` so a fetch failure surfaces. Suggested replacement:

    git fetch origin "$BASE_REF"
    changeset=$(git diff --name-only "origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD")

Discovered while unsticking the backlog of stranded PRs in li-6du.
title: ci.yml detect-py-changes step flakes with 'no merge base' on pull_request events

description:
## Why this issue exists

Supersedes li-7c0 (which framed the e2e-real workflow's silent test-skipping as a missing-secret problem). Discovered during the li-7c0 work: the deeper issue is that the 'real' tier of LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS is documented but UNIMPLEMENTED. tests/e2e/fake_claude.py runs identically in both 'mock' and 'real' modes — no code reads the env var, no claude-agent-sdk integration exists. Setting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY does NOT unlock live coverage; the workflow runs the mock harness regardless.

## What needs to be done

Implement the real-harness path:

1. Add a sibling 'real_claude.py' module (or extend fake_claude.py) that, when LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS=real, invokes claude-agent-sdk against the live Anthropic API to generate the per-sub-command payloads (instead of the deterministic mock payloads).
2. Each test in tests/e2e/test_*.py routes through a harness-selector function that dispatches to fake_claude.py vs real_claude.py based on the env var.
3. Update the just recipe + workflow to require ANTHROPIC_API_KEY when LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS=real (fail loudly when secret unset on master pushes; preserve fork-PR skip carve-out).
4. Verify on a test PR: workflow run actually calls the live API, produces real claude-agent-sdk traces in the run log.

## Cost concern

Live API calls per master push. With Haiku 4.5 and prompt caching, each test (4 tests in tests/e2e/) probably ~/usr/bin/zsh.05-0.20 of API spend. ~/usr/bin/zsh.20-0.80 per master push. Worth measuring before deciding whether to run on every push or restrict to weekly cron + workflow_dispatch.

## Acceptance criteria

- LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS=real produces materially different test execution from LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS=mock (network calls visible in workflow logs; claude-agent-sdk invocations measurable).
- e2e-real.yml CI run shows real API activity (Anthropic API call counts > 0).
- 'just e2e-test-claude-code-real' run locally exercises the live SDK path without needing test edits.
- Documentation in tests/e2e/CLAUDE.md, justfile, and e2e-real.yml is updated to remove the 'DORMANT / unimplemented' notices added by li-7c0's documentation pass.

## Out of scope

- Setting up monitoring / alerting on workflow failures (separate bead if needed).
- Selecting specific Anthropic model — pick Haiku for cost; Sonnet if Haiku is too weak for the multi-step skill flows.
- Whether to keep on every-master-push trigger vs. restrict (decide based on measured per-push cost).
reason: completed — PR #285 merged
title: Implement real harness in tests/e2e/ (claude-agent-sdk integration; supersedes li-7c0)

description:
## Why this issue exists

Supersedes li-abl. li-abl's first implementation (PR #78) shipped a workflow that fails on every PR due to the GITHUB_TOKEN permission limitation around enablePullRequestAutoMerge — see li-abl close-reason for the full diagnosis. PR #78 was reverted; this issue tracks the redo using GitHub-App-based auth.

Depends on the GitHub App + secrets being in place (see dependency).

## What needs to be done

Re-author .github/workflows/auto-enable-merge.yml using actions/create-github-app-token@v1 to mint a fresh installation token at runtime, then use that as GH_TOKEN for the gh pr merge --auto --rebase call.

Sketch:

    - uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v1
      id: app-token
      with:
        app-id: ${{ secrets.APP_ID }}
        private-key: ${{ secrets.APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}
    - env:
        GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
        REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
        PR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
      run: gh pr merge "$PR" --repo "$REPO" --auto --rebase

Keep the same trigger set, composed if-gate (NOT draft AND author in allowlist AND label list does NOT contain do-not-merge), per-PR concurrency group, and one-line warning-and-exit-1 on failure (App-token failure WOULD be a real signal worth surfacing).

## Acceptance criteria (carried from li-abl)

- After this workflow lands: opening a PR via gh pr create (no --auto flag, no --draft, no suppression label) results in the PR being set to auto-merge with rebase strategy within ~30 seconds of opening.
- A PR opened with --draft does NOT get auto-merge enabled.
- A PR opened with the do-not-merge label does NOT get auto-merge enabled.
- A PR opened by an unauthorized author does NOT get auto-merge enabled.
- The workflow's CI run shows GREEN on every PR (no false-failure pollution from the previous PR #78 design).
reason: Workflow .github/workflows/auto-enable-merge.yml restored via PR #81 (merge commit a4073c7) using GitHub-App-based auth (livespec-pr-bot, App ID 3668528). First test on PR #81 itself failed because the App's installation permissions only granted Pull requests: Read+Write — enablePullRequestAutoMerge ALSO needs Contents: Read+Write (the merge state touches branch state). App permissions widened to add Contents: Read+Write 2026-05-10T20:14Z; installation accepted the new permissions. Validated by the auto-merge behavior on this PR's close commit (this very PR).
title: Restore auto-merge gate workflow using GitHub-App-based auth (supersedes li-abl)

description:
## Why this issue exists

li-abl's first attempt (PR #78) shipped a workflow that uses GITHUB_TOKEN to call 'gh pr merge --auto --rebase'. That fails with 'GraphQL: Resource not accessible by integration (enablePullRequestAutoMerge)' — the GraphQL mutation requires admin-level repo access that github-actions[bot] does not have, regardless of declared permissions: pull-requests: write.

A fine-grained PAT would work but expires (max 1 year) and cannot be auto-rotated (GitHub's PAT-creation API requires user auth, not PAT auth — you can't bootstrap a new PAT from an existing one). For a 'set and forget' personal-account repo, GitHub Apps are the right answer: installation tokens auto-rotate every hour from a private key that has no calendar expiration.

## What needs to be done

1. Create a GitHub App in your personal account: Settings → Developer settings → GitHub Apps → New.
2. Configure the App with permission 'Pull requests: Read and write' on the livespec repo. No other scopes needed initially (we keep auto-update-branches.yml on the bot's GITHUB_TOKEN per the principle of least privilege; see li-abl close-reason).
3. Generate the App's private key (.pem); download it.
4. Install the App on thewoolleyman/livespec.
5. Store two repo secrets:
   - APP_ID: the numeric App ID from the App's settings page
   - APP_PRIVATE_KEY: contents of the downloaded .pem (paste verbatim, including BEGIN/END markers)
6. Verify the secrets exist via 'gh api repos/thewoolleyman/livespec/actions/secrets'.

## Acceptance criteria

- 'gh api repos/thewoolleyman/livespec/actions/secrets' lists APP_ID and APP_PRIVATE_KEY.
- A test workflow using actions/create-github-app-token@v1 + the two secrets can mint an installation token and successfully call the GraphQL enablePullRequestAutoMerge mutation against this repo.

## Notes

- Fine-grained PAT alternative was considered and rejected: cannot auto-rotate; risks silent breakage at expiration.
- This issue ONLY covers the App + secret setup. The actual workflow that uses the App lives in the dependent issue (auto-merge-redo).
- Existing auto-update-branches.yml continues to use GITHUB_TOKEN (which works for the update-branch REST endpoint). Not changed by this issue.
reason: GitHub App 'livespec-pr-bot' created (App ID 3668528, installation #131208965). Permissions: Pull requests Read+Write, Metadata Read (mandatory). Installation scoped to thewoolleyman/livespec only. Repo secrets APP_ID and APP_PRIVATE_KEY stored via 'gh secret set'. Local .pem shredded post-upload. App created via Playwright MCP browser session 2026-05-10.
title: Create GitHub App for repo automation (auto-merge etc.) with installation-token-based auth

description:
## Why this issue exists

The .github/workflows/e2e-real.yml workflow runs on every master push, but its first step ('Skip if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is unset') no-ops the actual test when the secret is missing. The workflow then reports SUCCESS — a canonical false-green: CI is green but the live claude-agent-sdk e2e test never runs.

Verified concretely 2026-05-10: most recent 5 runs on master all show:
- 'Skip if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is unset' → success (sets should_run=false)
- 'Install pinned binaries via mise' → SKIPPED
- 'Install Python dev deps via uv' → SKIPPED
- 'just e2e-test-claude-code-real' → SKIPPED
- Overall conclusion: SUCCESS

The repo currently has 0 stored secrets (gh api repos/thewoolleyman/livespec/actions/secrets returns empty). ANTHROPIC_API_KEY was never set up here, OR was removed at some point.

## Impact

Any regression in the live-claude path — schema drift between SKILL.md prompts and the SDK's actual behavior, prompt-formatting changes that break the e2e shape, vendored dependency incompatibilities — would land on master with green CI and not be caught until someone manually ran 'just e2e-test-claude-code-real' locally.

## What needs to be done — pick one

1. Set the secret: 'gh secret set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY --repo thewoolleyman/livespec'. Cheapest; restores actual e2e coverage immediately. Cost: api-key spend per master push.
2. Make the skip loud: change e2e-real.yml so the gate fails on master pushes when the secret is absent (only skip on PR-from-fork events). Forces the secret to be set or the workflow to be removed; no more silent-green.
3. Remove e2e-real.yml entirely if the live tests aren't worth running in CI — but document the decision so future-you doesn't reinstate it expecting it to run.

The original gate was added so PRs from forks don't fail on missing secrets. That carve-out is still defensible for fork PRs; the bug is that it ALSO no-ops master pushes.

## Acceptance criteria

After fix: master pushes either run the test (option 1) OR loudly fail (option 2) OR the workflow is gone (option 3). Silent-success-without-actually-testing must not recur.

## Source

Discovered 2026-05-10 while investigating li-abl's auth issue; the same gh-api-secrets check that surfaced the missing AUTO_MERGE_PAT also surfaced the missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
reason: Resolved as documentation pass — see PR for details. Original framing ('false-green from missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret') was correct in symptom but understated root cause: the real-harness tier is unimplemented (no code reads LIVESPEC_E2E_HARNESS; tests/e2e/fake_claude.py runs identically in both 'mock' and 'real' modes). Setting the secret would not unlock live coverage. Fix: documented the dormant state in justfile, .github/workflows/e2e-real.yml, and tests/e2e/CLAUDE.md so future readers don't expect live coverage that doesn't exist. Real-harness implementation tracked as li-949 (successor).
title: e2e-real workflow silently skips test when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset (false-green CI)

description:
## Why this issue exists

Live spec prose under SPECIFICATION/ carries pervasive references to the bootstrap authoring process that have outlived their purpose: "Phase 7 widening", "Phase 6 bootstrap-minimum", "Cycle NNN lands the X arm", "Per Plan Phase N sub-step ...". The bootstrap process closed at Phase 11 (per project CLAUDE.md and archive/) and these references no longer point to anything load-bearing — `archive/` holds the frozen historical artifacts, `SPECIFICATION/history/vNNN/` holds the per-revision audit trail. Carrying the residue in live spec prose creates two costs:

- **Reader-archaeology cost.** A new contributor reading "Phase 7 widens this to walk the template's full declared file manifest" must understand that "Phase 7" was a bootstrap-process phase, that Phase 7 already happened, that the referenced widening either landed or didn't, and then verify the current state — instead of just reading what the spec says today.
- **Bit-rot risk.** Every "Phase N widens X" forecast becomes wrong the moment X lands or ships differently. The spec drifts from reality every time a referenced phase completes.

This is distinct from the v050 §"Comment discipline" cleanup tracked under li-nhz: v050's scope-exemption explicitly excludes SPECIFICATION/** ("the spec IS the historical record"). v050 governs comments in scripts/, dev-tooling/, justfile, lefthook, workflows. THIS issue governs spec prose in SPECIFICATION/. Both cleanups share the same authoring-residue patterns but land via different mechanisms (v050 = direct edits per its constraint; THIS = governed propose-change/revise loop).

## What needs to be done

Restate every Phase-N / Cycle-NNN / Plan-§ / bootstrap-minimum reference in the affected spec files as a present-tense direct statement of the current rule. Examples of the rewrite shape:

- Before: "the gherkin-blank-line-format check (Phase 7 widening) MUST exempt minimal-rooted projects..."
- After: "the gherkin-blank-line-format check MUST exempt minimal-rooted projects..."

- Before: "Phase 6 lands bootstrap-minimum (1-2 properties per prompt); Phase 7 widens"
- After: rewrite as forward-looking "the catalogue currently carries N properties per prompt; future per-prompt regeneration cycles MAY widen via..." OR delete entirely if the widening has already happened.

- Before: "Pre-Phase-6 the check tolerated an empty [] array; from the Phase 6 seed forward (this revision and later), emptiness is a failure if any spec tree exists."
- After: "An empty [] array is a failure if any spec tree exists."

- Before: "Phase 3 lands the parser-only stub; Phase 7 widens it to the actual pruning mechanic."
- After: drop entirely; the spec should describe the current contract, not its authoring history.

## Affected files (12)

Live tree only; history/vNNN/ snapshots are correctly frozen and out of scope.

**Main spec (4 files, target SPECIFICATION/):**
- spec.md
- contracts.md
- constraints.md
- non-functional-requirements.md

**livespec sub-spec (4 files, target SPECIFICATION/templates/livespec/):**
- spec.md
- contracts.md
- constraints.md
- scenarios.md

**minimal sub-spec (4 files, target SPECIFICATION/templates/minimal/):**
- spec.md
- contracts.md
- constraints.md
- scenarios.md

## Recommended landing path

One /livespec:propose-change cycle per spec target (3 cycles total: main, livespec sub-spec, minimal sub-spec). Each proposed-change file enumerates the per-file rewrites for its target tree as one ## Proposal section per affected file (or as one bundled proposal if rewrites are uniform). /livespec:revise then lands the corrective edits per the standard governed loop.

Each cycle is mechanical (delete/rewrite per the regex hits) but each rewrite needs human judgment: is the Phase-N reference describing work that already happened (delete) or work still pending (restate as a forward-looking SHOULD/MAY)? The judgment call is per-occurrence; bulk find-replace would mangle the still-pending cases.

## Out of scope

- Comments and docstrings in `.claude-plugin/scripts/`, `dev-tooling/`, `justfile`, `lefthook.yml`, `.github/workflows/` — covered by li-nhz (v050 §"Comment discipline" implementation).
- archive/ — frozen historical artifacts.
- SPECIFICATION/history/vNNN/ — immutable per-revision snapshots.
title: Scrub Phase-N / bootstrap residue from live SPECIFICATION/ spec prose (12 files across 3 spec targets)

description:
## Why this issue exists

v050 cut SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md §"Comment discipline" (lines 577-589), which mandates two normative rules and two implementation deliverables. Only HALF of v050 was implemented (commit babc21e landed §"Pre-commit step ordering"); the comment-discipline half never landed. Today's auditable evidence:

- `dev-tooling/checks/comment_no_historical_refs.py` — does not exist (only `comment_line_anchors.py`, a different check, is present).
- Spec's reference regex `(?i)\b(v\d{3}\s*[A-Z]\d|per v\d{3}|phase\s+\d+|cycle\s+\d+|this commit|the previous (commit|PR))\b` returns 218 hits across the spec-named in-scope trees (`scripts/livespec/`, `scripts/bin/`, `dev-tooling/`, `justfile`, `lefthook.yml`, `.github/workflows/`). Should be 0 if the cleanup had run.
- The 218-hit baseline is contributing to ambient pollution flagged in this session's /livespec:doctor pass — Phase-N / Cycle-NNN pointers in module docstrings restate WHAT, reference the bootstrap Plan (now archived), or forecast future work that has since landed. None of these are WHY-form.

## What needs to be done

The spec mandates three concrete deliverables; this issue tracks all of them. Recommended ordering:

1. **Author `dev-tooling/checks/comment_no_historical_refs.py`** per v050 §"Enforcement". Greps every in-scope file for the historical-reference regex, exits non-zero with structured findings naming each violation site. Categorized as a python-code check per §"Pre-commit step ordering" (skipped on doc-only commits). Do NOT yet wire into `just check` — would block its own commit.

2. **Retroactive cleanup pass** per v050 §"Retroactive cleanup". Walk every Rule-1 (WHY-not-WHAT) and Rule-2 (no historical-bookkeeping) violation in the in-scope trees. Each comment is either rewritten to WHY-form (when carrying a still-relevant non-obvious WHY) or deleted (when pure historical bookkeeping or pure WHAT). Drive the regex hit count from 218 → 0. Rule 1 is judgment-based (code-review enforces); Rule 2 is mechanically grep-able.

3. **Wire the check into `just check`** alongside `check-comment-line-anchors`. After step 2 completes, the in-scope trees pass the check; CI stays green; future regressions are caught at pre-commit / pre-push / merge.

## Scope clarifications

- The retroactive cleanup is BIG — touches ~20 Python module docstrings + multiple justfile / lefthook / workflow comments. Will likely land as multiple PRs (one per package or per file group) rather than a single mega-PR.
- The spec's scope-exemption list applies: `_vendor/**`, `SPECIFICATION/**` (the spec IS the historical record), `SPECIFICATION/history/vNNN/`, `archive/`. The per-line escapes `# noqa: <CODE> — <reason>` and `# type: ignore[<code>] — <reason>` are already WHY-formed and remain compliant.
- A separate but related cleanup applies to live SPECIFICATION/*.md files for Phase-N / bootstrap-residue references (9 files). That belongs in a separate issue (the `non-functional-requirements.md` scope-exemption explicitly excludes `SPECIFICATION/`, so the v050 cleanup mandate does NOT cover spec prose). File separately if the user wants it tracked.
title: Implement v050 §'Comment discipline' — comment_no_historical_refs.py check + retroactive cleanup

description:
# Auto-merge gate workflow — author-and-state gated

## Why this issue exists

PRs that pass CI currently sit open until someone manually
invokes `gh pr merge --auto --rebase` (or merges directly).
Today's session (PR #56) demonstrated the failure mode: the
agent created the PR, CI went green, and nothing merged it
until the user noticed and asked. The same pattern recurs any
time an agent opens a PR but forgets the auto-merge incantation,
or the user opens a PR and walks away.

The natural fix is a GitHub Actions workflow that sets
auto-merge automatically when the PR author is the user (or an
agent acting via the user's identity) AND the PR is not a draft
AND not flagged for human review.

Complementary to li-x0x (auto-update-branches.yml race
condition for BEHIND PRs) — that issue keeps PRs from sticking
in BEHIND after master moves; this issue ensures they enter
the auto-merge state in the first place. Both are needed for
the rebase-merge-only discipline (per
non-functional-requirements.md §"Commit and merge discipline")
to be hands-off.

## Proposed workflow shape

`.github/workflows/auto-enable-merge.yml`:

- **Trigger**: `pull_request` event types `opened`, `reopened`,
  `ready_for_review`, `synchronize`, `unlabeled`.
- **Job condition (composed)**:
  - `pull_request.draft == false`
  - `pull_request.user.login` in author allowlist (initially:
    `thewoolleyman`; extensible for agent bot accounts if any
    are added later)
  - `pull_request.labels` does NOT contain `do-not-merge`
    (or equivalent suppression label — name TBD)
- **Action**: `gh pr merge ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} --auto --rebase`
- **Token**: `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `pull-requests: write` permission
  is enough; auto-merge doesn't require a PAT.

The author allowlist gates the behavior naturally:
- User's own PRs (gh CLI / web UI) → auto-merge enabled
- Agent-opened PRs (Claude Code, etc., using user's PAT) →
  auto-merge enabled (author == user)
- External bot PRs (dependabot, future renovate, etc.) →
  NO auto-merge unless added to the allowlist
- Future collaborator PRs → NO auto-merge unless added to the
  allowlist

The `do-not-merge` label and `--draft` give explicit opt-out
when the user wants a "open it, think for a beat, then merge
manually" rhythm.

## Acceptance criteria

- After this workflow lands: opening a PR via `gh pr create`
  (no `--auto` flag, no `--draft`, no suppression label) results
  in the PR being set to auto-merge with rebase strategy within
  ~30 seconds of opening.
- A PR opened with `--draft` does NOT get auto-merge enabled
  (verified by inspecting `gh pr view --json autoMergeRequest`).
- A PR opened with the `do-not-merge` label does NOT get
  auto-merge enabled.
- A PR opened by an unauthorized author (test via temporarily
  spoofing or by a separate test account) does NOT get
  auto-merge enabled.
- Existing PR #56-style stall does not recur in normal use.

## Possible NFR sibling (worth flagging)

This implementation is the mechanism. The corresponding spec
rule — "PRs whose author is in the user's identity allowlist
AND not draft AND not flagged MUST auto-rebase-merge as soon
as CI passes" — could land via `/livespec:propose-change` to
`non-functional-requirements.md` §"Commit and merge discipline"
if you want the rule to bind future workflows / agents (not
just this one workflow file). Mirror of li-1f5's pattern: spec
rule (Items 1-4) + code mechanism (Items 5-6).

## Source

Filed during livespec session 2026-05-10 after PR #56 merged
manually because the agent (me) opened the PR without
`gh pr merge --auto --rebase`. User asked whether all PRs
should auto-merge when green, then asked whether the gate
could be limited to user/agent-opened PRs. This issue captures
the agreed shape.

description:
# Fix auto-update-branches.yml race condition

## Observation

`.github/workflows/auto-update-branches.yml` fires only on
`push: branches: [master]`, runs once when the merge completes, and
at that exact moment a freshly-queued PR may still be in BLOCKED
state (CI in flight). After CI finishes and the PR's state
transitions to BEHIND, no event re-triggers the workflow — so
the PR sits stuck indefinitely until someone manually triggers
`gh api -X PUT /pulls/{N}/update-branch`.

Concrete repro from 2026-05-10: PRs #52 and #53 queued in quick
succession, both with `gh pr merge --auto --rebase`. PR #52
merged at 03:20:21Z, triggering the workflow. Workflow run
25618596577 logs show:

> No PRs behind master.

at 03:20:24Z — PR #53 was still BLOCKED at that moment. PR #53
transitioned to BEHIND minutes later and sat stuck for ~3 hours
until manually unstuck.

## Why this matters

This recurs whenever two or more PRs are queued close together
on a personal-account repo (no GitHub Merge Queue available;
that's an org-only feature). Without an auto-rebase mechanism
that catches PRs as they BECOME behind (not just at master push),
the rebase-merge-only discipline (per
non-functional-requirements.md §"Commit and merge discipline")
requires manual intervention every time multiple PRs land in
sequence.

## Fix — add both triggers for redundancy

Add two additional triggers to the existing workflow:

1. **`schedule: cron`** — e.g. every 5 minutes. Catches any PR
   that drifted into BEHIND for any reason (race condition,
   manual edits, etc.). Cheap; no-op when nothing's behind.
2. **`workflow_run: completed`** on the main CI workflow
   (`ci.yml`). When CI finishes on a PR, re-evaluate that PR's
   merge state — this catches the race-condition path directly,
   right when the BLOCKED → BEHIND transition can happen.

Keep the existing `push: branches: [master]` trigger. Concurrency
group already exists; the additional triggers won't deadlock
because `cancel-in-progress: false`.

## Acceptance criteria

- After merging two PRs in quick succession via `gh pr merge
  --auto --rebase`, the second PR auto-rebases and merges within
  ~5 minutes (cron) or as soon as its CI completes
  (workflow_run), without manual `update-branch` intervention.
- Existing single-PR-merge path continues to work (push trigger
  unchanged).

## Source

Filed during livespec session 2026-05-10. Manually unstuck PR #53
via `gh api -X PUT repos/thewoolleyman/livespec/pulls/53/update-branch`
after observing the race; this issue captures the underlying
fix so the workaround isn't needed next time.
reason: Race condition fix landed via PR #74 (merge commit af76e69). Added schedule:cron + workflow_run:CI triggers to .github/workflows/auto-update-branches.yml; existing push trigger preserved. Acceptance is observational (next two-PRs-in-quick-succession case); implementation is in place.
title: Fix auto-update-branches.yml race condition — PR can stick in BEHIND after master push

description:
# Enforcement mechanisms for agent-collaboration discipline

Six items emerged from a session-end reflection on three observed
failure modes during PR #42's bootstrap (bd auto-commit on master,
vague `/schedule` gesturing, audit table living only in chat
transcript). The reflection is captured in
`research/workflow-processes/spec-vs-implementation-line.md`
(produced in the same conversation).

Items 1–4 are **spec content (non-functional requirements)** —
they MUST flow through `/livespec:propose-change` →
`/livespec:revise` per the spec's own self-application rule. Each
will likely be authored as a separate themed proposal, or rolled
into the broader auto-memory migration's themed proposals (see
related issue li-jdq), depending on the user's preferred grouping
when migration starts.

Items 5–6 are **code/config (mechanism that realizes the spec
rules)** — they land via normal commits, ideally as gap-tied
work once items 1–4 are in non-functional-requirements.md (so
refresh-gaps surfaces them as gaps, plan creates beads issues
for them, implement closes them).

The user explicitly requested this issue be filed clearly
indicating the spec/code split.

## The six items

### 1. Spec rule — destructive-default CLI wrapping

Destructive-default CLIs (those whose default behaviour writes,
commits, deletes, or otherwise mutates state without explicit
opt-in) MUST be invoked through a project-local scripted wrapper
(just recipe / shell script) that pins safe flags. Direct CLI
invocation by agents is discouraged in agent-facing prose.

Failure mode this addresses: bd init's auto-commit-to-master
during the bootstrap session. Setup-beads.sh already enforces
this for bd specifically; the rule generalises.

**Path:** `/livespec:propose-change` →
`SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/<topic>.md` →
`/livespec:revise` → lands in
`SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md`.

### 2. Spec rule — verify before referring

Agents MUST verify a tool / skill / feature exists in the current
environment AND does what they claim before referring to it by
name in agent prose. No vague capability-name-dropping; if
uncertain, the agent says \"I'd need to verify whether <X>
applies here\" rather than offering it as a path.

Failure mode this addresses: the assistant suggested
`/schedule` as a follow-up mechanism without confirming it fit
the situation (it doesn't — `/schedule` triggers on date/time, not
conditions like \"after PR #42 merges\").

**Path:** same as item 1 — propose-change → revise → NFR.

### 3. Spec rule — completion includes workspace cleanup, not just artifact landing

Substantial work artifacts (audit tables, multi-step plans,
decision matrices, migration breakdowns, anything whose value
extends beyond the current chat turn) MUST be persisted (filed
as a beads issue, committed to a tracked file, etc.) before the
message containing them is sent. Chat-only delivery is reserved
for ephemeral, single-decision analysis.

**Completion ALSO includes restoring workspace state.** When
work goes through intermediate states that aren't the natural
post-completion home (feature branches during PR work,
checked-out hotfix branches, modified working trees), the
agent's \"done\" state MUST include explicit cleanup back to the
expected post-completion baseline:

- After a PR merges: switch back to master, fast-forward pull,
  delete the local feature branch, delete the remote branch
  (unless \`gh pr merge --delete-branch\` already did so during
  merge). Don't tell the user the commands and call it complete
  — run them.
- After a destructive intermediate (e.g., \`git reset --hard\` to
  recover from an unwanted state): the agent's \"done\" state is
  the recovered + re-validated state, not the reset itself.
- \"I'll leave you to verify\" is acceptable for verification
  steps that genuinely require user judgment (visual UI checks,
  subjective output review). It is NOT acceptable as a stand-in
  for mechanical workspace hygiene the agent could just perform.

Failure modes this addresses:
- The auto-memory migration audit (42-entry breakdown into 9
  themed proposals) was produced as a chat message and would
  have been lost on conversation compaction had the user not
  pushed back.
- Three PRs landed during PR #42 / #43 / #44's session
  (bootstrap, marketplace fix, bd-workarounds catalogue). After
  each merge, the agent left the user on the merged feature
  branch and typed instructions to run \`git checkout master &&
  git pull\` rather than running them. Branch cleanup (local +
  remote) was also deferred as instructions, not actions.

**Path:** same as item 1 — propose-change → revise → NFR.

### 4. Spec rule — beads state-change consent boundary

Agents MUST NOT perform state-changing bd writes on the user's
behalf without explicit, per-operation user consent. This covers
every bd command that mutates issue state:

- \`bd create\` — filing a new issue
- \`bd update <id> --status in_progress\` — claiming work to do
- \`bd update <id> --description/--title/--notes/...\` — non-trivial
  body edits beyond cosmetic field changes
- \`bd close <id>\` — closing an issue (with or without --resolution)
- \`bd dep add <a> <b>\` — adding a dependency edge

Every such invocation requires the user to confirm the specific
operation (which issue, what change) before it's executed. Auto-
filing / auto-claiming / auto-closing — including \"I'll file
these as I go\", \"let me batch-file the follow-ups\", or \"I'll
pick the next ready issue\" — is forbidden. The consent boundary
is per-operation, not per-session. Asks must NOT pressure-load
the consent (e.g., \"while we're in this session, so they don't
get lost\"); the right framing is \"Here's the operation, here's
the scope. Yes/no?\" without urgency tail.

Failure modes this addresses:
- Agent tendency to suggest batch-filing beads issues for
  follow-up work, eroding the consent boundary that should
  govern every persistent artifact (PR #42 / #43 / #44 sessions).
- Agent tendency to auto-claim a ready issue when invoked via
  /livespec-implementation-beads:implement (session 2026-05-10:
  agent ran \`bd update li-0d0 --status in_progress\` based on
  the SKILL.md's \"pick the next leaf-level issue\" directive,
  without first asking the user which of the seven ready issues
  to work on).

The mechanism that realizes this rule for the implement / plan
SKILL.md prose is tracked separately in li-dou (gap-0005). The
SKILL.md expansion MUST encode the candidate-surfacing + per-
operation user-confirm step so the directive language can't be
reparsed as autonomous-claim instructions.

**Path:** same — propose-change → revise → NFR.

### 5. Code — \`dev-tooling/checks/no_direct_destructive_cli.py\`

A new dev-tooling check that greps \`dev-tooling/\`,
\`.claude-plugin/\`, and \`.claude/plugins/\` for direct invocations
of known-destructive-default CLIs (\`bd init\`, \`git push --force\`,
\`git reset --hard\`, \`gh repo delete\`, etc. — list curated
explicitly) outside an explicit allowlist. Fails CI on
violation. Implements item 1 mechanically.

**Path:** normal commit. Ideally tracked as a gap-tied beads
issue once item 1 lands in NFR — refresh-gaps would surface
the missing enforcement check as a gap, plan creates an issue,
implement closes it via standard Red-Green flow.

### 6. Code — Claude Code \`Stop\` hook for plan-artifact detection

A hook in \`.claude/settings.json\` (under \`Stop\`) that scans the
agent's last turn for substantial planning artifacts (markdown
tables above a row threshold, lists above an item threshold,
heading-decorated planning content describing future work) and
surfaces a warning to the user IF no recent tool call in that
turn persisted equivalent content. The hook NEVER auto-files;
it only surfaces the missed persistence. Implements item 3
mechanically.

Could optionally extend to surface workspace-cleanup gaps too:
detect when the agent declares a task \"complete\" while still
on a non-master branch with merged history, or with a dirty
working tree, and surface the gap.

**Path:** normal commit. Same gap-tied path as item 5.

## Sequencing

1. Wait for PR #42 to merge into master. (DONE — merged
   2026-05-09T19:25:18Z, commit 9a90fb3.)
2. Land items 1–4 via \`/livespec:propose-change\` →
   \`/livespec:revise\` (likely grouped with li-jdq's themed
   proposals — items 2, 3, 4 in particular align with li-jdq's
   Proposal 2 \"Agent-decision discipline\").
3. After NFR has the rules: refresh-gaps surfaces items 5–6 as
   gaps; plan creates beads issues; implement closes them via
   Red-Green flow.

## Open question NOT in this list

There's a deeper workflow-process question raised by the user
that this six-item list does NOT yet answer: where exactly is
the line between spec content and implementation-only intent,
and what's the workflow for the latter? Captured separately in
\`research/workflow-processes/spec-vs-implementation-line.md\` for
further thought before any formalization.

## Update history

- 2026-05-09 (initial filing): six items captured per user
  request.
- 2026-05-09 (Item 3 expansion): broadened from \"persist plan
  artifacts\" to \"completion includes workspace cleanup\". User
  observed pattern across PR #42 / #43 / #44 — agent kept
  typing checkout-master-and-pull instructions instead of just
  running them. Workspace-cleanup belongs in the same NFR rule
  because the underlying failure is identical (treating chat
  text as a substitute for the mechanical action).
- 2026-05-10 (Item 4 expansion): broadened from \"bd create only\"
  to \"any state-changing bd write on the user's behalf\" — covers
  bd update --status (claim), bd update --description (body
  edits), bd close, bd dep add. Title changed from \"beads-
  filing consent boundary\" to \"beads state-change consent
  boundary\". Failure mode observed: in session 2026-05-10 the
  agent auto-claimed li-0d0 via \`bd update --status in_progress\`
  when invoked through /livespec-implementation-beads:implement,
  parsing the SKILL.md's \"pick the next leaf-level issue\"
  directive as autonomous-claim instructions. Cross-reference:
  li-dou (gap-0005) tracks the SKILL.md expansion that
  mechanically realizes this rule for the implement / plan
  skills.
title: Enforcement mechanisms for agent-collaboration discipline (4 spec rules + 2 code/config items)

# Migrate auto-memory entries into SPECIFICATION/ via propose-change/revise

## Why this issue exists

The user does not want per-machine auto-memory entries
(`~/.claude/projects/-data-projects-livespec/memory/`). All
project rules are functional or non-functional requirements and
must therefore flow through the spec lifecycle:
`/livespec:propose-change` → `/livespec:revise`. AGENTS.md /
per-directory CLAUDE.md hold orientation prose only, never
load-bearing rules.

This issue captures the audit table assembled during the bootstrap
session on 2026-05-09 so the migration can resume from a
persistent record (the table only existed in chat transcript
before this issue was filed).

## Sequencing

This work starts AFTER PR #42 (the bootstrap PR) merges into
master. Then on a fresh branch (`spec/migrate-auto-memory-to-nfr`
or similar), each themed proposal below is authored as
`SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/<topic>.md` per the existing
format. Selective-revise (v052) lets multiple proposals be
processed in one or several `/livespec:revise` passes.

## Already-spec'd entries — DELETE without migration (7)

These memory entries duplicate content already in the spec or
config files; no propose-change needed.

| # | Memory entry | Already covered by |
|---|---|---|
| 3 | Enforcement suite is invocation-agnostic | NFR §Contracts §"Toolchain pins" + justfile-as-source-of-truth |
| 5 | livespec uses mise for tool versions | NFR §Contracts §"Toolchain pins" + .mise.toml |
| 6 | UV is the Python toolchain manager | NFR §Contracts §"Toolchain pins" + pyproject.toml |
| 9 | Task runner is single source of truth | NFR §Contracts (justfile delegates) |
| 11 | Per-sub-command skill structure | spec.md (seven /livespec:* skills) + NFR §Contracts §"Project-local implementation plugin command surface" |
| 22 | tmp/ ownership | project conventions + .gitignore carve-out |
| 40 | Revise selective proposal processing | landed in v052 |

## Themed propose-changes (35 entries → 9 topics)

### Proposal 1 — Skill dialogue discipline
Target: new NFR §Spec or §Constraints subsection
- #1 one question at a time in interviews
- #4 always recommend in interviews
- #7 prefer exploring standardized libraries (option-presentation)
- #19 honest pushback over reflexive agreement
- #21 only ask when there's genuine ambiguity
- #26 one investigation, one finding, one question
- #30 questions are not directives

### Proposal 2 — Agent-decision discipline (autonomous execution)
Target: new NFR §Constraints subsection
- #24 fast-forward execution mode
- #32 plan-prescribed git ops don't need separate auth gates
- #35 verify load-bearing contracts before briefing
- #36 don't halt on simple typos
- #37 write-existing-file requires fresh Read before parallel writes

### Proposal 3 — Code-architecture rules (Python authoring)
Target: NFR §Constraints — augment existing or add new subsections
- #8 strongest-possible guardrails for agent-authored Python
- #10 static enumeration over dynamic discovery
- #13 domain errors vs bugs in ROP-style specs
- #14 specify architecture, not mechanism
- #15 minimize new dependencies; reuse vendored parsers
- #17 keyword-only args + keyword-only match patterns
- #18 user-provided extensions get minimal requirements
- #20 mocks comply with livespec rules

### Proposal 4 — Test discipline
Target: NFR §Constraints — augment existing testing rules
- #31 tests for cwd-fallback branches must monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
- #34 flaky tests are unacceptable

### Proposal 5 — Commit / PR / branch discipline
Target: NFR §Constraints §"Commit and merge discipline" (already exists; extend)
- #27 commits represent cohesive units of value, not RGR phases
- #33 use `mise exec --` for git operations that fire lefthook
- #38 phase advance is a PR boundary
- #39 master red is not a deferral (surface at session-start)

### Proposal 6 — Spec lifecycle additions (propose-change / revise / critique)
Target: NFR §Constraints — augment lifecycle/skill mechanics sections
- #2 don't obsess over prior iterations during brainstorming
- #12 brainstorming deferred-items discipline
- #23 bootstrap halts on any plan/PROPOSAL inconsistency
- #25 severity judgment over rule-following on PROPOSAL drift
- #28 verify PROPOSAL.md drift via literal-text grep
- #29 committed zip beats gitignored directory for archive-but-don't-peek

### Proposal 7 — Documentation audience and writing style
Target: new NFR §Constraints subsection
- #16 reframed: documentation and agent explanations MUST treat Python familiarity as a non-default reader audience (the rule is about output style; the per-person fact "user X is not a Python dev" is NOT a project requirement and is dropped). DECISION POINT before authoring this proposal.
- #41 spell out "non-functional-requirements"; never abbreviate as NFR

### Proposal 8 — Auto-memory ban (the meta-rule)
Target: new NFR §Constraints subsection "Project rule discoverability" or similar
- New: agents MUST NOT use Claude Code's auto-memory feature in this project; all project rules live in SPECIFICATION/. If a memory directory exists locally, treat it as deprecated and surface to the user for migration.

### Proposal 9 — Implementation-layer hardening (extends the v058 section)
Target: NFR §Spec / §Constraints "Repo-local implementation workflow" / "Beads invariants"
- #42 bd init MUST use `--skip-agents --skip-hooks` (and setup-beads.sh enforces this)

## Concrete next steps

1. After PR #42 merges: `git checkout master && git pull`
2. `git checkout -b spec/migrate-auto-memory-to-nfr`
3. For each Proposal above, run `/livespec:propose-change` and author the file under `SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/<topic>.md`.
4. Run `/livespec:revise` (selective per-proposal) to land them.
5. After acceptance, delete the corresponding memory entries (and the 7 already-covered deletions in the table above).
6. Once Proposal 8 lands, the auto-memory ban is enforced by the spec itself and any residual memory entries are flagged as deprecated artifacts.

## Decision point worth flagging at proposal-authoring time

For Proposal 7: confirm whether #16 should be reframed as a documentation-audience rule (recommended — applies to all output style) or simply dropped (because per-user preferences aren't project requirements). Don't author the proposal until decided.
reason: PR #264 (master at 24aef04) filed SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/migrate-auto-memory-project-architecture-entries.md, migrating the 3 project_*.md auto-memory entries that codify persistent architectural commitments (tmp/ ownership, research/workflow-processes/ tool-agnostic-vs-implementation split, vendored-lib pyright resolution discipline). Per the 2026-05-26 orchestrator brief at tmp/prompt.md Wave 2, this work-item's scope was narrowed to 'file the PC for project-architecture entries'; the broader 9-themed-proposal plan in this work-item's original description was deliberately superseded — feedback_*.md and user_*.md auto-memory entries are agent-collaboration preferences and user-role profile (NOT project architectural commitments) and remain in user memory rather than migrating to spec. The 5 project_*.md entries deliberately not migrated are enumerated in PR #264's body: 3 already covered by existing spec at §Enforcement-suite invocation / §Toolchain pins, 2 are completion-log entries (li-xxjopf, li-nhz) that belong in git log per 'Spec is for contracts, not tracking'. A follow-on /livespec:revise pass against the PC accepts or rejects the 3 findings at file-level review time.

description:
# Workflow note for plan.py and implement.py: JSONL update batching

## Observation

During the bootstrap session, every standalone `bd update` /
`bd create` (e.g., editing an issue body, filing a freeform
issue) produces a `.beads/issues.jsonl` diff that needs to land
on master via the normal PR cycle. For one-off updates this is
heavyweight — a single-line JSONL diff goes through feature
branch + lefthook gates + PR + auto-merge + branch deletion (~5
distinct steps for ~1 byte of meaningful change).

PR #45 was a concrete example: editing li-1f5's body to expand
Item 3 produced one full PR cycle for a one-line JSONL diff.

## Why this matters for plan.py / implement.py

Both skills do many bd writes during their normal flow:
- `plan.py` may file a batch of issues for newly-discovered
  gaps, plus add `bd dep` edges between them.
- `implement.py` does `bd update --status in-progress`, then
  several `bd update --description` updates as work progresses,
  then `bd close --resolution fix --note "<audit fields>"` at
  closure.

If each bd write triggers its own PR cycle, the implementation
loop becomes unworkable. Per-issue work would generate 5–10
PRs each.

## Suggested workflow shape

Standalone bd updates that don't have a natural carrier PR
(like li-1f5 body edits) get their own micro-PR — accept the
overhead. But within a normal workflow:

- `plan.py` does its batch of `bd create` + `bd dep add` ops,
  then commits the auto-exported `.beads/issues.jsonl` once at
  the end of the planning session as a single
  `chore(beads): plan batch — li-N..li-M` commit on the
  planning branch. Single PR for the batch.
- `implement.py` does its bd writes (status, descriptions,
  close) within the gap-closure feature branch. The final
  JSONL update rides into the gap's PR alongside the code
  changes that closed the gap — single commit, single PR for
  the gap.
- Standalone bd updates outside a workflow (manual ad-hoc
  edits, like li-1f5 body expansion) land via micro-PR.

This is the natural answer; encode it in the SKILL.md prose
once the Python automation is being written so the bias toward
"batch within a workflow" is explicit, not discovered the hard
way.

## Status

- This issue is freeform (no `gap-id:` label) — it's an
  observation, not a tracked spec gap.
- Cross-references the relevant gap-tied issues:
  - li-bxm (gap-0002, plan.py)
  - li-37p (gap-0003, implement.py)
- No `bd dep` edges — this is a "consider when implementing"
  note, not a blocker. Reading order is up to whoever picks up
  li-bxm or li-37p.

## Source

Surfaced during PR #45's session-end discussion when the user
observed that going through full PR cycle for every bd update
is unsustainable. Captured before the conversation ends so it
doesn't live only in transcript.
title: Note for implement.py / plan.py: batch JSONL updates rather than per-bd-update PRs

description:
See implementation-gaps/current.json gap-0005. Once gap-0001/2/3 close and Python automation exists, expand each SKILL.md to reference now-existing entry points and remove 'Manual fallback (current state)' sections.

## Acceptance criteria for the implement SKILL.md expansion

The expanded prose MUST include an explicit user-confirmation step before claiming any beads issue:

1. After running `bd ready --json`, surface the candidate issues to the user (compact table or list with id, priority, gap-id label, title).
2. Ask the user which issue to work on. Do NOT auto-pick based on priority / dependent-count / lower-id heuristics.
3. Only after the user confirms a specific issue does the skill run `bd update <id> --status in_progress`.

This addresses a failure mode observed in session 2026-05-10: the stub SKILL.md's "pick the next leaf-level issue" directive was parsed as autonomous-claim instructions, and the agent claimed li-0d0 without asking. The user-pick step belongs in the SKILL.md itself, not just in the human's expectation.

Cross-reference: li-1f5 Item 4 codifies the underlying spec rule (consent before any state-changing bd write on the user's behalf); this issue is the SKILL.md mechanism that realizes it for the `implement` skill.

## Acceptance criteria for the plan SKILL.md expansion

Same principle for `bd create` and `bd dep add` operations: present each proposed issue (and each proposed dep edge) to the user for per-issue consent before filing. This is the SKILL.md realization of li-1f5 Item 4's existing wording on `bd create`.

## Acceptance criteria for the refresh-gaps SKILL.md expansion

description:
## Goal

Extract the shared enforcement-suite scripts currently in livespec-core's
`dev-tooling/checks/` into a new sibling repo `livespec-dev-tooling`,
governed by livespec via its own SPECIFICATION/ tree, tracked by
livespec-impl-plaintext, and consumed by livespec-core + every
livespec-impl-* repo as (a) a versioned Python package (uv git source)
and (b) composite GitHub Actions + reusable workflows.

## Trigger

`just bootstrap` failed in livespec-impl-plaintext (2026-05-20) because
templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja's bootstrap recipe references
`dev-tooling/git-hook-wrapper.sh` but the template never ships a
`dev-tooling/` subtree. Root-cause investigation revealed the broader
gap: copier (per livespec/SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Shared content
sync — copier template") is the *sole* shared-content mechanism, and
copier alone cannot deliver shared *code* — only static scaffolds. The
39 scripts in livespec-core/dev-tooling/checks/ are referenced by the
impl-plugin template's justfile but are not delivered to consumers.
This epic addresses the structural gap; the bootstrap bug is the most
visible symptom.

## Architectural decision

Create `github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling`. The repo:

1. **Is itself governed by livespec.** Empty SPECIFICATION/ seeded via
   `/livespec:seed` using the `livespec` 4-file template
   (spec.md / contracts.md / constraints.md / scenarios.md plus
   non-functional-requirements.md). Livespec's first dogfood at the
   *library* scale — distinct from the plugin scale (livespec-core)
   and the impl-plugin scale (livespec-impl-plaintext). If anything
   breaks (e.g., heading taxonomy fits awkwardly), that's free signal
   for livespec to refine.

2. **Tracks its own work via livespec-impl-plaintext.** `.livespec.jsonc`
   declares `implementation: { plugin: livespec-impl-plaintext }` with
   a `compat` block pinning livespec-core. Same pin-and-bump shape
   every livespec-governed repo follows per livespec-core
   contracts.md §"Cross-repo coordination — pin-and-bump".

3. **Ships a Python package.** Modules at
   `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/<slug>.py` invocable as
   `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>`. Consumers add via
   uv git source — no PyPI initially:

       [tool.uv.sources]
       livespec-dev-tooling = { git = "https://github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling.git", tag = "v0.1.0" }

   PyPI is a future optional flip; uv git source gives tag-pinned
   reproducible builds today.

4. **Ships composite Actions + reusable workflows** at `.github/actions/`
   and `.github/workflows/reusable-*.yml`. Consumer CI calls
   `uses: thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling/.github/actions/<name>@v0.1.0`.

5. **Defines its own contracts.** contracts.md spec's:
   - Each check's argv contract and exit-code semantics
     (0 = pass; non-zero = fail with structured stderr).
   - "No breaking change to a check's CLI without a major version
     bump" invariant.
   - "No network IO from a check" invariant.
   - Python runtime floor (3.10, matching livespec-core).
   - The semver-stable surface vs. internal-implementation distinction.

6. **Hand-author the project scaffold once.** `templates/impl-plugin/`
   doesn't fit (it ships Claude Code plugin manifests). YAGNI on
   creating `templates/library/` until a second library variant appears.

## Composition with other defenses (out of scope here)

This epic delivers options C+D from the architectural brainstorm
(extracted package + reusable Actions). The following options are
*complementary, not redundant* and should be filed as separate
work-items:

- **Option A** — behavioral smoke test extending livespec-core's
  `dev-tooling/checks/copier_template_smoke.py` to actually run
  `just bootstrap && just check` inside the tmpdir-generated tree.
  Catches the original bootstrap-bug class at livespec-core CI time.
  Can land independently of this epic.
- **Option E** — propose-change cycle codifying Option A as a
  MUST-level contract in livespec-core contracts.md + branch
  protection. Depends on A.
- **Option F** — each impl repo's CI runs `just bootstrap` from a
  clean checkout. Defense in depth. Files per impl repo.

This epic's scope is *only* extracting the package + Actions and
rewiring consumers.

## Sub-task decomposition (file each as a depends_on'd work-item)

When picking this epic up, decompose into these sub-tasks in order
(each is one PR; chain them via `depends_on`):

**G.1 — propose-change cycle: spec amendment in livespec-core**
   - `/livespec:propose-change` against livespec-core
     SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Shared content sync — copier
     template" to declare livespec-dev-tooling as the shared-code
     mechanism alongside copier; name the pin-and-bump relationship
     for it; enumerate which checks are "shared across livespec-
     governed projects" vs "livespec-core-private."
   - Same proposal MAY also touch non-functional-requirements.md
     §"Shared content provenance" to name the new repo.
   - `/livespec:revise` to land vNNN.

**G.2 — hand-author livespec-dev-tooling repo scaffold**
   - Create github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling.
   - Hand-author (no copier): pyproject.toml, justfile, lefthook.yml,
     .mise.toml, .python-version, .github/workflows/ci.yml,
     .github/workflows/release-please.yml, release-please-config.json,
     .release-please-manifest.json, .livespec.jsonc (template:
     livespec, implementation: livespec-impl-plaintext, compat
     block pinning livespec-core), empty livespec_dev_tooling/
     package, empty tests/ mirror, README.md skeleton.
   - Versions/configs must match livespec-core for consistency.

**G.3 — `/livespec:seed` the new repo**
   - Run `/livespec:seed` with the `livespec` template.
   - Pre-seed dialogue answers anchor on:
     - spec.md: "shared enforcement-suite library for livespec-
       governed projects; semver-stable CLI surface; no runtime
       livespec dependency."
     - contracts.md: per-check argv/exit-code contracts; the
       `python -m` invocation surface; composite-action input/output
       contracts; reusable-workflow inputs/secrets/outputs.
     - constraints.md: no network IO; runtime floor 3.10; semver
       discipline; no Python 3.12+ syntax.
     - scenarios.md: livespec-core consumes via uv git source;
       livespec-impl-plaintext consumes via `python -m`; CI consumes
       via reusable workflow.
     - non-functional-requirements.md: coverage gate 100% per-file;
       1:1 paired tests; ruff/pyright/coverage configs matching
       livespec-core; comment discipline.
   - Snapshot v001.

**G.4 — classify and migrate the 39 existing checks**
   - Classify each livespec-core/dev-tooling/checks/<slug>.py as
     **shared** (move) or **livespec-core-private** (stays). The
     classification itself is a propose-change against livespec-dev-
     tooling's new contracts.md.
   - Likely livespec-core-private: `schema_dataclass_pairing.py`,
     `copier_template_smoke.py`. Everything else likely shared.
   - For each shared check, one PR in livespec-dev-tooling:
     move the script, move the paired test, refactor for
     importability (most are already cwd-anchored — fine), add to
     the package's __init__.py exports if needed.
   - Group PRs by check family (style, coverage, AST, CI-alignment,
     red-green-replay) for reviewer load — exact grouping is the
     agent's call at pickup time.

**G.5 — composite Actions + reusable workflows**
   - `.github/actions/setup/action.yml` — checkout + mise install + uv sync.
   - `.github/actions/run-check/action.yml` — `uv run python -m
     livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>` parameterized by inputs.
   - `.github/workflows/reusable-check-matrix.yml` — matrix-strategy
     workflow consumers `uses:` to run the shared-check suite.
   - Tag v0.1.0.

**G.6 — wire livespec-core to consume the package**
   - Add `livespec-dev-tooling @ git+...@v0.1.0` to
     `[dependency-groups].dev` via `[tool.uv.sources]`.
   - Delete moved scripts from livespec-core/dev-tooling/checks/;
     keep only livespec-core-private checks.
   - Update livespec-core's justfile targets to invoke
     `uv run python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>`.
   - Update livespec-core's `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to
     `uses:` the reusable workflow where applicable.
   - `just check` must pass.
   - Tag livespec-core next semver.

**G.7 — wire livespec-impl-plaintext + impl-plugin template**
   - Same shape as G.6 against livespec-impl-plaintext.
   - Update livespec-core's `templates/impl-plugin/pyproject.toml.jinja`
     to declare livespec-dev-tooling as a dev-dep with `[tool.uv.sources]`.
   - Update `templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja` so every reference
     to `dev-tooling/checks/<slug>.py` becomes
     `uv run python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>`.
   - Ship `git-hook-wrapper.sh` either (a) verbatim in the template's
     `dev-tooling/` subtree, OR (b) as a `livespec_dev_tooling.bin`
     resource installed by the package. (b) is cleaner; choose at
     implementation time.
   - Run `copier update` in livespec-impl-plaintext to pull the new
     template.
   - `just bootstrap && just check` must pass in livespec-impl-plaintext.
   - This phase fixes the original bootstrap-bug trigger as a
     side-effect.

## Acceptance criteria

1. livespec-dev-tooling v0.1.0+ exists at the canonical URL with its
   own seeded SPECIFICATION/ tree dogfooded via livespec-impl-plaintext.
2. livespec-core's `just check` passes consuming the package
   (no orphan dev-tooling/checks/ entries; clean import boundary).
3. livespec-impl-plaintext's `just bootstrap && just check` passes
   consuming the package.
4. `templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja` no longer references any
   script the template doesn't ship — every reference resolves to a
   `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>` invocation backed
   by the templated `pyproject.toml.jinja` dependency.
5. The original `just bootstrap` failure in livespec-impl-plaintext
   is fixed as a side-effect (verify by removing any local workaround
   and re-running).

## Spec changes required

- livespec-core/SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Shared content sync —
  copier template" — amend (Phase G.1).
- livespec-core/SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md §"Shared
  content provenance" — possibly amend (same propose-change).
- livespec-dev-tooling/SPECIFICATION/* — authored from scratch via
  `/livespec:seed` (Phase G.3).

## Non-goals

- PyPI publishing (uv git source is sufficient initially; later flip).
- Creating `templates/library/` at livespec-core (YAGNI until a second
  library appears).
- Option A behavioral smoke (separate work-item).
- Option E spec-driven smoke discipline (separate work-item, depends
  on A).
- Option F impl-side defense-in-depth (separate per-impl-repo
  work-items).

## References

- Bug trigger: `just bootstrap` failure in livespec-impl-plaintext
  on 2026-05-20.
- livespec-core/SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Shared content sync —
  copier template".
- livespec-core/SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Cross-repo coordination
  — pin-and-bump".
- livespec-core/SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md §"Shared
  content provenance".
- livespec-impl-plaintext `.livespec.jsonc` (the `compat` block shape
  the new repo mirrors).
- uv git source docs: `[tool.uv.sources]` with `git = "..."` +
  `tag = "..."`.
- Existing scaffold for reference: livespec-core/templates/impl-plugin/
  (impl-plugin scaffold; not used directly here but informs the
  hand-authored library scaffold).

visible symptom.

## Architectural decision

Create `github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling`. The repo:

1. **Is itself governed by livespec.** Empty SPECIFICATION/ seeded via
   `/livespec:seed` using the `livespec` 4-file template
   (spec.md / contracts.md / constraints.md / scenarios.md plus
   non-functional-requirements.md). Livespec's first dogfood at the
   *library* scale — distinct from the plugin scale (livespec-core)
   and the impl-plugin scale (livespec-impl-plaintext). If anything
   breaks (e.g., heading taxonomy fits awkwardly), that's free signal
   for livespec to refine.

2. **Tracks its own work via livespec-impl-plaintext.** `.livespec.jsonc`
   declares `implementation: { plugin: livespec-impl-plaintext }` with
   a `compat` block pinning livespec-core. Same pin-and-bump shape
   every livespec-governed repo follows per livespec-core
   contracts.md §"Cross-repo coordination — pin-and-bump".

3. **Ships a Python package.** Modules at
   `livespec_dev_tooling/checks/<slug>.py` invocable as
   `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>`. Consumers add via
   uv git source — no PyPI initially:

       [tool.uv.sources]
       livespec-dev-tooling = { git = "https://github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling.git", tag = "v0.1.0" }

   PyPI is a future optional flip; uv git source gives tag-pinned
   reproducible builds today.

4. **Ships composite Actions + reusable workflows** at `.github/actions/`
   and `.github/workflows/reusable-*.yml`. Consumer CI calls
   `uses: thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling/.github/actions/<name>@v0.1.0`.

5. **Defines its own contracts.** contracts.md spec's:
   - Each check's argv contract and exit-code semantics
     (0 = pass; non-zero = fail with structured stderr).
   - "No breaking change to a check's CLI without a major version
     bump" invariant.
   - "No network IO from a check" invariant.
   - Python runtime floor (3.10, matching livespec-core).
   - The semver-stable surface vs. internal-implementation distinction.

6. **Hand-author the project scaffold once.** `templates/impl-plugin/`
   doesn't fit (it ships Claude Code plugin manifests). YAGNI on
   creating `templates/library/` until a second library variant appears.

## Composition with other defenses (out of scope here)

This epic delivers options C+D from the architectural brainstorm
(extracted package + reusable Actions). The following options are
*complementary, not redundant* and should be filed as separate
work-items:

- **Option A** — behavioral smoke test extending livespec-core's
  `dev-tooling/checks/copier_template_smoke.py` to actually run
  `just bootstrap && just check` inside the tmpdir-generated tree.
  Catches the original bootstrap-bug class at livespec-core CI time.
  Can land independently of this epic.
- **Option E** — propose-change cycle codifying Option A as a
  MUST-level contract in livespec-core contracts.md + branch
  protection. Depends on A.
- **Option F** — each impl repo's CI runs `just bootstrap` from a
  clean checkout. Defense in depth. Files per impl repo.

This epic's scope is *only* extracting the package + Actions and
rewiring consumers.

## Sub-task decomposition (file each as a depends_on'd work-item)

When picking this epic up, decompose into these sub-tasks in order
(each is one PR; chain them via `depends_on`):

**G.1 — propose-change cycle: spec amendment in livespec-core**
   - `/livespec:propose-change` against livespec-core
     SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Shared content sync — copier
     template" to declare livespec-dev-tooling as the shared-code
     mechanism alongside copier; name the pin-and-bump relationship
     for it; enumerate which checks are "shared across livespec-
     governed projects" vs "livespec-core-private."
   - Same proposal MAY also touch non-functional-requirements.md
     §"Shared content provenance" to name the new repo.
   - `/livespec:revise` to land vNNN.

**G.2 — hand-author livespec-dev-tooling repo scaffold**
   - Create github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling.
   - Hand-author (no copier): pyproject.toml, justfile, lefthook.yml,
     .mise.toml, .python-version, .github/workflows/ci.yml,
     .github/workflows/release-please.yml, release-please-config.json,
     .release-please-manifest.json, .livespec.jsonc (template:
     livespec, implementation: livespec-impl-plaintext, compat
     block pinning livespec-core), empty livespec_dev_tooling/
     package, empty tests/ mirror, README.md skeleton.
   - Versions/configs must match livespec-core for consistency.

**G.3 — `/livespec:seed` the new repo**
   - Run `/livespec:seed` with the `livespec` template.
   - Pre-seed dialogue answers anchor on:
     - spec.md: "shared enforcement-suite library for livespec-
       governed projects; semver-stable CLI surface; no runtime
       livespec dependency."
     - contracts.md: per-check argv/exit-code contracts; the
       `python -m` invocation surface; composite-action input/output
       contracts; reusable-workflow inputs/secrets/outputs.
     - constraints.md: no network IO; runtime floor 3.10; semver
       discipline; no Python 3.12+ syntax.
     - scenarios.md: livespec-core consumes via uv git source;
       livespec-impl-plaintext consumes via `python -m`; CI consumes
       via reusable workflow.
     - non-functional-requirements.md: coverage gate 100% per-file;
       1:1 paired tests; ruff/pyright/coverage configs matching
       livespec-core; comment discipline.
   - Snapshot v001.

**G.4 — classify and migrate the 39 existing checks**
   - Classify each livespec-core/dev-tooling/checks/<slug>.py as
     **shared** (move) or **livespec-core-private** (stays). The
     classification itself is a propose-change against livespec-dev-
     tooling's new contracts.md.
   - Likely livespec-core-private: `schema_dataclass_pairing.py`,
     `copier_template_smoke.py`. Everything else likely shared.
   - For each shared check, one PR in livespec-dev-tooling:
     move the script, move the paired test, refactor for
     importability (most are already cwd-anchored — fine), add to
     the package's __init__.py exports if needed.
   - Group PRs by check family (style, coverage, AST, CI-alignment,
     red-green-replay) for reviewer load — exact grouping is the
     agent's call at pickup time.

**G.5 — composite Actions + reusable workflows**
   - `.github/actions/setup/action.yml` — checkout + mise install + uv sync.
   - `.github/actions/run-check/action.yml` — `uv run python -m
     livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>` parameterized by inputs.
   - `.github/workflows/reusable-check-matrix.yml` — matrix-strategy
     workflow consumers `uses:` to run the shared-check suite.
   - Tag v0.1.0.

**G.6 — wire livespec-core to consume the package**
   - Add `livespec-dev-tooling @ git+...@v0.1.0` to
     `[dependency-groups].dev` via `[tool.uv.sources]`.
   - Delete moved scripts from livespec-core/dev-tooling/checks/;
     keep only livespec-core-private checks.
   - Update livespec-core's justfile targets to invoke
     `uv run python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>`.
   - Update livespec-core's `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to
     `uses:` the reusable workflow where applicable.
   - `just check` must pass.
   - Tag livespec-core next semver.

**G.7 — wire livespec-impl-plaintext + impl-plugin template**
   - Same shape as G.6 against livespec-impl-plaintext.
   - Update livespec-core's `templates/impl-plugin/pyproject.toml.jinja`
     to declare livespec-dev-tooling as a dev-dep with `[tool.uv.sources]`.
   - Update `templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja` so every reference
     to `dev-tooling/checks/<slug>.py` becomes
     `uv run python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>`.
   - Ship `git-hook-wrapper.sh` either (a) verbatim in the template's
     `dev-tooling/` subtree, OR (b) as a `livespec_dev_tooling.bin`
     resource installed by the package. (b) is cleaner; choose at
     implementation time.
   - Run `copier update` in livespec-impl-plaintext to pull the new
     template.
   - `just bootstrap && just check` must pass in livespec-impl-plaintext.
   - This phase fixes the original bootstrap-bug trigger as a
     side-effect.

## Acceptance criteria

1. livespec-dev-tooling v0.1.0+ exists at the canonical URL with its
   own seeded SPECIFICATION/ tree dogfooded via livespec-impl-plaintext.
2. livespec-core's `just check` passes consuming the package
   (no orphan dev-tooling/checks/ entries; clean import boundary).
3. livespec-impl-plaintext's `just bootstrap && just check` passes
   consuming the package.
4. `templates/impl-plugin/justfile.jinja` no longer references any
   script the template doesn't ship — every reference resolves to a
   `python -m livespec_dev_tooling.checks.<slug>` invocation backed
   by the templated `pyproject.toml.jinja` dependency.
5. The original `just bootstrap` failure in livespec-impl-plaintext
   is fixed as a side-effect (verify by removing any local workaround
   and re-running).

description:
auto-update-branches.yml uses default GITHUB_TOKEN to call `gh api PUT /pulls/{N}/update-branch`. GitHub's recursion rule: pushes authored by GITHUB_TOKEN do NOT fire downstream workflows. Result: every auto-updated PR head ends up with zero CI check-runs, and master's strict required_status_checks leaves the PR permanently BLOCKED+MERGEABLE.

Same class as li-8f3 (auto-enable-merge needed App token because GITHUB_TOKEN couldn't enable auto-merge). Same fix: mint a GH App installation token via actions/create-github-app-token@v1 and use that token for the update-branch call. The header comment in auto-update-branches.yml currently argues for keeping GITHUB_TOKEN per least-privilege, but overlooks that the workflow-recursion ceiling applies to its push, not just to auto-merge mutations.

Observed 2026-05-11: PRs #59 #60 #63 #64 #65 #86 all stranded BLOCKED+MERGEABLE with 0 check-runs on head SHA. Unstuck by close+reopen from PAT (triggers CI). PRs #61 #62 were also stranded but superseded — closed.

Secondary bug (file separately if desired): ci.yml detect-py-changes uses `git diff origin/master...HEAD` but actions/checkout on pull_request events doesn't reliably create origin/master remote-tracking ref; 3/6 reruns hit `fatal: origin/master...HEAD: no merge base`. Fix: explicit `git fetch origin master` (full depth — fetch-depth: 0 is already set in checkout) before the diff.
reason: PR #162 merged 2026-05-23. auto-update-branches.yml now uses a GitHub App installation token; pushes are treated as external and fire downstream CI. PRs no longer strand BLOCKED+MERGEABLE after auto-update.
title: auto-update-branches uses GITHUB_TOKEN — pushes don't trigger CI, stranding PRs

description:
ci.yml's `detect-py-changes` step runs:

    git fetch origin "$BASE_REF" --depth=1 2>/dev/null || true
    changeset=$(git diff --name-only "origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD")

On `pull_request` events, actions/checkout@v4 does not reliably create the `origin/master` remote-tracking ref even with `fetch-depth: 0` — the shallow `--depth=1` fetch can land without a common ancestor reachable, and the diff fails with:

    fatal: origin/master...HEAD: no merge base
    ##[error]Process completed with exit code 128.

Observed 2026-05-11: 3 of 6 close+reopen-triggered CI runs (#64, #65, #86) failed here on first attempt; rerunning the same run with no code change passed. Pure flake.

Fix: drop the `--depth=1` (fetch-depth: 0 already pulls full history in the checkout) and remove the silent `|| true` so a fetch failure surfaces. Suggested replacement:

    git fetch origin "$BASE_REF"
    changeset=$(git diff --name-only "origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD")

Discovered while unsticking the backlog of stranded PRs in li-6du.
reason: Fixed alongside li-6du in PR #162 + follow-up PR #163. ci.yml detect-py-changes dropped --depth=1 (the root cause of 'no merge base' against shallow fetches) and kept `|| true` as a defensive fallback for fetch failures.
title: ci.yml detect-py-changes step flakes with 'no merge base' on pull_request events
