description:
## Goal

After epic `li-fgqgnk` (G.1-G.7) ships, extract livespec-dev-
tooling's hand-authored toolchain scaffold into a sibling copier
template at `livespec-core/templates/library/`. Mirrors how
`templates/impl-plugin/` factors shared scaffolding for
`livespec-impl-*` consumers.

## Trigger

Phase G.2 (li-sfwahb) hand-authored livespec-dev-tooling's
scaffold per the epic's YAGNI on `templates/library/`. This
follow-up tracks the duplication-management debt that decision
created. As of G.2 the duplication surface across livespec-core,
livespec-impl-plaintext, and livespec-dev-tooling is:

- ruff config (~150 lines, 27 categories) — identical across
  all three.
- pyright config (strict + 7 strict-plus diagnostics) — identical.
- coverage config (100% line+branch fail_under) — identical.
- pytest config (minversion, addopts, importlib mode) — identical
  modulo per-repo pythonpath.
- mise pins (uv 0.5.20, just 1.36.0, lefthook 1.13.6) — identical.
- .python-version (3.10.16) — identical.
- lefthook.yml shape (three-stage pre-commit, commit-msg
  no-commit-on-master + red-green-replay, pre-push full
  aggregate) — identical modulo per-repo `run:` target slugs.
- justfile shape (`check` aggregate, `check-pre-commit`
  Red-mode-aware, `check-pre-push` zero-py subsetting, etc.)
  — substantially identical.
- CI workflow shape (per-target matrix, zero-py subsetting,
  unconditional metadata matrix) — identical modulo target
  list per repo.

Drift between these scaffolds (when livespec-core adopts a new
ruff rule, a new lefthook stage, etc.) becomes manual mirror-work
in the absence of a template.

## Architectural decision (proposed)

Add `livespec-core/templates/library/` as a sibling copier
template to `livespec-core/templates/impl-plugin/`. Templated
files cover the duplication-prone surface (pyproject.toml.jinja
with installable-package shape, justfile.jinja, lefthook.yml.jinja,
.mise.toml.jinja, .python-version.jinja, ci.yml.jinja,
release-please.yml.jinja + configs). Library-specific concerns
(package name, runtime dependencies, importlinter contracts once
architecture stabilizes) become copier variables.

## Spec amendments required

1. `livespec-core/SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` §"Shared content
   sync — copier template" — generalize the template enumeration
   from `templates/impl-plugin/` only to also list
   `templates/library/`. Same propose-change MAY also amend
   §"Shared code sync — livespec-dev-tooling" to cross-reference
   the library template.
2. `livespec-core/SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md`
   §"Shared content provenance" — explicitly state that
   sibling-library static scaffolds flow via the
   `templates/library/` copier template (mirroring the
   impl-plugin path).

## Execution sketch

1. `/livespec:propose-change` the spec amendments above.
2. `/livespec:revise` to land them.
3. Author `livespec-core/templates/library/` files by extracting
   the common shape from livespec-dev-tooling's hand-authored
   scaffold.
4. Run `copier copy gh:thewoolleyman/livespec/templates/library
   <tmpdir>` against the new template and verify the output
   matches livespec-dev-tooling's current scaffold byte-
   identically modulo intended copier-variable substitutions.
5. Backfill livespec-dev-tooling with a `.copier-answers.yml`
   tracking the template version.
6. Wire livespec-dev-tooling's CI to run `copier update --dry-run`
   for drift detection (mirroring impl-plugin consumers).
7. Author `check-copier-template-smoke.py` covering the new
   template (and migrate it via livespec-dev-tooling so every
   livespec-governed repo runs it in CI).

## Dependency

Depends on G.7 (li-nyxtnb) completion — Phase G.7 finishes the
epic; this follow-up extracts the template only after
livespec-dev-tooling's scaffold has at least one bump-pin PR
landed against it (i.e., the scaffold has been touched by at
least one downstream-driven change, so the extracted template
captures a stabilized shape rather than the initial-author
guess).

## Non-goals

- Retroactively templating livespec-core's own scaffold (it
  predates this template and stays hand-authored unless a
  separate effort migrates it).
- Sub-spec sub-templates for libraries (out of scope for v1 of
  `templates/library/`).
title: Extract templates/library/ for livespec-governed sibling libraries (post-G.7 follow-up to epic li-fgqgnk)

description:
## Goal

Refactor every shared check in livespec_dev_tooling/checks/ to read its source-tree paths from configuration rather than hardcoded constants. This unlocks (a) restoring fail_under=100 on coverage by un-skipping the three tests skipped in G.4's migration commit, (b) livespec-dev-tooling self-application against its own tree, and (c) livespec-impl-* consumers running the checks against their own non-livespec-core layouts.

## Background

Phase G.4 of epic li-fgqgnk (commit c7d9603 in livespec-dev-tooling) migrated 34 shared checks AS-IS. Their hardcoded constants like `_LIVESPEC_TREE = Path('.claude-plugin') / 'scripts' / 'livespec'` still match livespec-core's layout exactly, so livespec-core (Phase G.6 consumer) works immediately. But other consumers (livespec-impl-plaintext at G.7, livespec-dev-tooling itself for self-application) get silent-no-op behavior from any check whose default tree doesn't exist in their layout.

Three tests are explicitly skipped pending this work:

- test_branch_protection_alignment::test_real_repo_passes — the check hardcodes `_REPO_OWNER_REPO = 'thewoolleyman/livespec'`.
- test_check_coverage_incremental::test_resolve_mirror_test_for_dev_tooling_checks_pair — the function maps livespec-core's sourceâtest mirror schema.
- test_check_coverage_incremental::test_main_passes_against_fully_covered_real_repo_pair — invokes the check against `dev-tooling/checks/all_declared.py` which doesn't exist in livespec-dev-tooling's layout.

## Design sketch

Introduce a config-file convention: each consumer's pyproject.toml (or, fallback, an env var like LIVESPEC_DEV_TOOLING_SOURCE_TREES with colon-separated paths) declares the source trees the checks walk. Each check reads the config at startup; falls back to its historical livespec-core defaults if no config is present. `branch_protection_alignment.py` reads the repo identifier from `git remote get-url origin` (parsing the github.com URL) rather than the hardcoded constant.

## Acceptance

1. All 34 shared checks accept a source-tree config and fall back to livespec-core defaults when none is provided.
2. livespec-dev-tooling's pyproject.toml declares a `[tool.livespec_dev_tooling].source_trees = ["livespec_dev_tooling"]` entry and `just check` runs every check against the library's own tree (self-application per SPECIFICATION/constraints.md §"Self-application").
3. The three skipped tests are restored (`@pytest.mark.skip` removed); coverage gate restored to `fail_under = 100`.
4. livespec-impl-plaintext's pyproject.toml (Phase G.7) similarly declares its own source-trees; its `just check` exercises the shared checks against its own tree.
5. livespec-core's pyproject.toml (Phase G.6) either declares source-trees explicitly OR relies on the historical defaults; either way, livespec-core's `just check` stays bit-identical to the pre-G.6 baseline.

## Dependency

Depends on G.7 (li-nyxtnb) completion. Until G.7 lands, the consumers are not yet rewired and the refactor has no validation surface beyond livespec-dev-tooling itself.
title: Path-portability refactor for livespec-dev-tooling checks (restore 100% coverage + self-application + cross-consumer use)

description:
Implement the no-stale-gap-tied cleanup invariant per SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Doctor cross-boundary invariants" → §"no-stale-gap-tied".

## Contract recap

A gap-tied open work-item whose underlying gap no longer surfaces in a fresh impl-plugin gap-detection run MUST be closed via a non-fix disposition (`spec-revised`, `no-longer-applicable`, `resolved-out-of-band`, or equivalent administrative reason). The check fires `warn` (NOT `fail`) when an open gap-tied work-item's gap-id is absent from a fresh detection run, with narration directing the user to close the item via the appropriate non-fix path.

## Why this is more involved than the other cleanup invariants

The three cleanup invariants landed under li-f5wmjr (no-stale-merged-branch, no-stale-merged-pr-branch, no-stale-worktree) each interrogate git/gh state directly. no-stale-gap-tied requires invoking the active impl-plugin's gap-detection in a NON-MUTATING dry-run mode at check time. `capture-impl-gaps` as authored is interactive (per-rule user yes/no), so a separate machine surface is required.

## Architectural decision needed before implementing

Pick ONE of:

A. Add a `detect-gaps --json` thin-transport surface to livespec-impl-plaintext that scans the spec via Spec Reader, applies the MUST/SHOULD regex, and emits gap-ids as JSON without filing or asking. Doctor invokes it via subprocess. Cleanest separation; mirrors `list-work-items` pattern.

B. Extend `capture-impl-gaps` with a `--dry-run --json` mode that skips Steps 2-3 (per-rule classification + per-gap filing) and emits the candidate gap-ids only. Reuses the existing skill scaffold but mixes the interactive and machine paths in one skill.

C. Reimplement the gap detector inside `livespec/doctor/static/no_stale_gap_tied.py` without subprocess. Couples doctor to the gap-detection regex directly. Violates the impl-plugin abstraction boundary.

Recommended: A (thin-transport surface).

## Sub-tasks

1. Spec amendment: livespec-impl-plaintext/SPECIFICATION/contracts.md MUST declare the new thin-transport surface (input/output contract for `detect-gaps --json`).
2. Impl: livespec-impl-plaintext exposes the `detect-gaps --json` command + skill (likely a thin authored skill mirroring `list-work-items`).
3. Bump-pin: livespec consumes the new impl-plaintext via its compat entry.
4. Doctor invariant: `livespec/.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/no_stale_gap_tied.py` implementing the warn-on-stale rule. Pattern follows `no_stalled_epic.py`: read `.livespec.jsonc`, resolve the active impl-plugin, subprocess the new `detect-gaps` surface, parse the JSON gap-ids, compare against open gap-tied work-items in the JSONL store.
5. Tests: 100% coverage per file. Mock the subprocess seam.
6. Registry: register `doctor-no-stale-gap-tied` in `livespec/.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/__init__.py`.

## Acceptance

- doctor static phase emits a `doctor-no-stale-gap-tied` finding (pass / skipped / warn) per run.
- Real-spec test: open a gap-tied work-item whose gap_id was deleted from the spec → check fires warn.
- The check is skipped when the active impl-plugin is not in the supported set (mirroring `no_stalled_epic`'s `_SUPPORTED_PLUGINS` guard).
- All sub-tasks land in dependency order (1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6).

## Context links

- SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Doctor cross-boundary invariants" → §"no-stale-gap-tied".
- Implementation pattern: `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/no_stalled_epic.py`.
- Impl-plugin gap detection: `livespec-impl-plaintext/.claude-plugin/skills/capture-impl-gaps/SKILL.md` (current interactive flow).
title: Implement no-stale-gap-tied doctor cross-boundary invariant

description:
## Why this issue exists

`just check-types` (`uv run pyright`) currently reports **2,423 errors across 65 files** on master and is explicitly EXCLUDED from the `just check` aggregate per justfile lines 84-94: "+ `check-lint`/`check-format`/`check-types` (deferred until config-tier-fix cycles land)". `check-lint` and `check-format` have since rejoined the aggregate, but `check-types` remains deferred — meaning pyright errors silently accumulate and new code (e.g., `dev-tooling/checks/comment_no_historical_refs.py` from li-nhz Step 1) inherits the broken baseline without any signal at commit, pre-push, or CI time.

This violates the project principle that "checks must run properly and stay that way".

## Root cause

102 of the 2,423 errors are `reportMissingImports` for the five vendored libraries under `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/` (structlog, returns, jsoncomment, fastjsonschema, typing_extensions). Runtime code adds `_VENDOR_DIR` to `sys.path` at import time:

```python
_VENDOR_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[N] / ".claude-plugin" / "scripts" / "_vendor"
if str(_VENDOR_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(_VENDOR_DIR))

import structlog  # noqa: E402
```

Pyright performs static analysis and never executes that path-injection, so `structlog` resolves as Unknown. That cascades into the remaining ~2,300 errors: `log = structlog.get_logger(...)` → Unknown variable type → every subsequent `log.error(...)` call has Unknown return + Unknown member access, generating `reportUnknownMemberType`, `reportUnknownVariableType`, `reportUnknownArgumentType`, etc., per call site.

## Error-category breakdown (2026-05-24 baseline)

| Count | Diagnostic |
|---|---|
| 678 | reportUnknownMemberType |
| 610 | reportUnknownVariableType |
| 426 | reportUnknownLambdaType |
| 243 | reportUnknownArgumentType |
| 170 | reportUnknownParameterType |
| 102 | reportMissingImports |
| 57  | reportPrivateUsage |
| 46  | reportArgumentType |
| 26  | reportUntypedFunctionDecorator |
| 25  | reportAttributeAccessIssue |
| 20  | reportImplicitStringConcatenation |
| 8   | reportUnusedImport |
| 4   | reportUnusedFunction |
| 4   | reportUnusedCallResult |
| 2   | reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment |

The top six categories (~2,229 errors) are all downstream of the 102 `reportMissingImports` root failures. Once vendored imports resolve, most cascading errors should disappear.

## Design approach

**Step 1: Resolve vendored imports.** Add `extraPaths = [".claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor"]` to `[tool.pyright]` in pyproject.toml. This makes pyright find the vendored libs the same way runtime does. Expected impact: the 102 `reportMissingImports` go away; most of the ~2,229 cascade errors collapse.

**Step 2: Address residual Unknown\* errors.** If vendored libs lack type annotations (likely for structlog/returns/jsoncomment), the per-import Unknown member/variable errors will persist. Options:
- Install the upstream PyPI package as a dev-only typing dep (pyright sees stubs, runtime still uses vendored copy). Requires careful PyPI-vs-vendor version alignment.
- Add a vendored `.pyi` stub file alongside each vendored package.
- Mark vendored libs as `stubPath` targets and author project-local stubs.

**Step 3: Fix residual non-vendor issues.** `reportPrivateUsage` (57), `reportImplicitStringConcatenation` (20), `reportAttributeAccessIssue` (25), `reportArgumentType` (46), `reportUntypedFunctionDecorator` (26), `reportImplicitOverride` errors are independent of the vendor issue and need targeted fixes per call site. Some may be config-tunable (relax the strict-plus diagnostic to warning for specific subsystems).

**Step 4: Rejoin `check-types` to the `just check` aggregate.** Add it to the targets list in justfile, alongside check-lint and check-format. Update the deferred-checks comment to reflect that check-types is now active.

**Step 5: Add CI workflow gate.** Verify check-types is in the .github/workflows/ci.yml job matrix so pre-merge gating fires.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `uv run pyright` exits 0 against master.
2. `just check` aggregate includes `check-types` and passes end-to-end.
3. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` exercises `check-types` as a required check on every PR.
4. The deferred-checks comment in justfile lines 84-94 is updated to reflect that check-types has rejoined (or removed entirely if no checks remain deferred).
5. Any structural carve-outs needed (e.g., `# type: ignore[<code>] — <reason>` on lines where the vendored lib API truly cannot be typed) are explicit and reasoned in the comment per SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md §"Typechecker constraints".

## Scope estimate

- Step 1 is one-line config change; likely fixes 2,000+ errors mechanically.
- Step 2 is the largest unknown — depends on stub availability for vendored libs. Could be 1 PR (extraPaths only) up through multi-PR (custom stubs per vendor).
- Step 3 is a per-call-site cleanup; likely 50-200 small fixes across the codebase.
- Step 4-5 are mechanical wire-in.

Best handled as a multi-PR epic. The first PR can land Step 1 alone to validate the cascade hypothesis; subsequent PRs land Steps 2-5 incrementally with the error count monotonically dropping each PR.

## Cross-cutting note

Future check-* deliverables added to the suite (e.g., li-nhz Step 1 from PR #189) inherit this broken baseline. Once this epic lands, those new checks will need a follow-up review to verify they pass check-types cleanly against the new gating.
title: Restore check-types to just check aggregate — resolve 2,423-error pyright baseline (vendored-imports cascade + residuals)

description:
## Why this issue exists

Captured originally as memo `mm-gzi7ej` (2026-05-23). doctor's out-of-band-edits auto-backfill writes into v(N+1) where N is read from git HEAD, but during a just-completed revise's post-step the working tree already has v(N+1) cut from the in-flight revise — so the auto-backfill files land INSIDE that just-cut directory instead of v(N+2), clashing with the legitimate revision files.

## Symptoms observed

Observed during v072 cut (2026-05-23): `cross-repo-dependency-awareness-revision.md` correctly placed in v072/proposed_changes/, but doctor's post-step auto-backfill then wrote `out-of-band-edit-2026-05-23t16-41-35z-{.md,-revision.md}` into the SAME v072 directory because it computed N=71 from git HEAD (where v072 isn't committed yet). The auto-backfill also produced an inconsistent revision file (claimed README.md changed but byte-content was identical to v071's README.md), triggering doctor-accept-decision-snapshot-consistency to fail on the snapshot it just wrote.

## Proposed fix

The auto-backfill should use working-tree state (or post-revise state) to determine the next version slot, not git HEAD. Alternative: skip the auto-backfill when running as the post-step of a just-completed revise (the revise already absorbed the drift into v(N+1)).

## Pointers

- `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/_out_of_band_edits_writes.py`
- `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/out_of_band_edits.py`
reason: Bug was already fixed by commit d89e0da on master (`fix(doctor): scan past working-tree v-dirs for next OOB slot (mm-gzi7ej)`) BEFORE the memo was dispositioned in PR #226. The mm-gzi7ej memo was captured in commit 07f217a but never closed via process-memos; PR #226's auto-disposition didn't verify the fix had already landed.
title: doctor out-of-band-edits auto-backfill writes to v(N+1) using git HEAD instead of working-tree state

description:
## Why this issue exists

Captured originally as memo `mm-8xcq3s` (2026-05-24). Seed wrapper (`bin/seed.py`) REWRITES `.livespec.jsonc` to a minimal `{"template": "..."}` form on every invocation, clobbering any hand-authored content (compat blocks, `implementation` key, `cross_repo_targets`, user comments).

## Symptoms observed

Observed during livespec-runtime sibling-library bootstrap (`/livespec:seed` against pre-authored `.livespec.jsonc` with compat metadata). Worked around in livespec-runtime PR #3 by `git checkout .livespec.jsonc` after the wrapper exits.

## Proposed fix

Seed wrapper should MERGE template-required keys (`template`, `spec_root`) into the existing config rather than overwriting the whole file. Sibling libraries needing pre-authored config (compat, cross_repo_targets, implementation) are increasingly common — this bug blocks the canonical sibling-bootstrap flow.
reason: Fixed by commit 9329b4f (PR #227, `fix(seed): preserve pre-existing .livespec.jsonc verbatim (li-2qjqen)`). The fix in `_write_livespec_config` skips the write when the file already exists, preserving pre-authored sibling-library configs verbatim. Acceptance criterion (pre-authored config preserved byte-for-byte) verified by `test_seed_main_preserves_existing_livespec_jsonc_with_compat_metadata`.
title: seed wrapper clobbers pre-authored .livespec.jsonc on every invocation (blocks sibling-bootstrap with compat metadata)

## Why this issue exists

Captured originally as memo `mm-3orvuu` (2026-05-23). Investigate adding a Claude Code hook (UserPromptSubmit or PreToolUse, configured via `settings.json`) that intercepts any attempt by the agent to save observations into Claude Code's local auto-memory (`~/.claude/projects/.../memory/` Write tool calls) and routes them through `/livespec-impl-plaintext:capture-memo` instead.

## Motivation

Local auto-memory is per-machine + per-user transient state, lost when the project is cloned elsewhere or when memory is pruned; `memos.jsonl` is checked in, durable, and flows through the canonical `process-memos` triage (spec-bound → propose-change, impl-bound → work-item, persistent-knowledge → store, discard). Observed 2026-05-23: agent twice reached for local auto-memory when the user-correct path was `capture-memo` — user explicitly redirected both times.

## Open investigation questions

- (a) Which hook event fires earliest in the Write-tool path so the hook can substitute the call rather than just blocking it?
- (b) Should the hook fire on every Write to `*/memory/*.md` or only when the agent's free-text observation pattern is detected?
- (c) Should the hook be livespec-plugin-scoped (fires only when project carries `.livespec.jsonc` declaring an impl-plugin with `memos_path`) or globally on?
- (d) How to surface the substitution to the user so the agent's intent stays visible?

## Related

Belt-and-suspenders complement to the capture-memo SKILL.md discoverability gaps (originally memo `mm-h3fulw`, owned by livespec-impl-plaintext sibling repo).
title: Investigate Claude Code hook to redirect agent local auto-memory writes through capture-memo

description:
## Why this issue exists

Captured originally as memo `mm-3orvuu` (2026-05-23). Investigate adding a Claude Code hook (UserPromptSubmit or PreToolUse, configured via `settings.json`) that intercepts any attempt by the agent to save observations into Claude Code's local auto-memory (`~/.claude/projects/.../memory/` Write tool calls) and routes them through `/livespec-impl-plaintext:capture-memo` instead.

## Motivation

Local auto-memory is per-machine + per-user transient state, lost when the project is cloned elsewhere or when memory is pruned; `memos.jsonl` is checked in, durable, and flows through the canonical `process-memos` triage (spec-bound → propose-change, impl-bound → work-item, persistent-knowledge → store, discard).

## Investigation findings (2026-05-25)

All four open questions resolved:

**(a) Earliest hook event**: PreToolUse fires before Write tool execution. Returns `{"decision": "block", "reason": "..."}` on stdout (exit 0) to reject the call and surface the reason in the agent's transcript. Cannot transparently substitute the call (agent must re-issue), but rejection + explanation is sufficient for routing the agent to capture-memo.

**(b) Trigger discipline**: fire on every Write whose `file_path` matches `**/memory/*.md` (Claude Code's auto-memory path pattern). No content-pattern detection needed — the path itself is the canonical signal.

**(c) Scoping**: livespec-plugin-scoped via hook script logic (NOT plugin-config conditional). Hook script reads `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` env var, checks for `.livespec.jsonc`, parses `memos_path`. If absent: exit 0 (allow Write); if present: emit block-decision. This keeps the hook installable globally via the plugin while only activating in livespec-governed projects.

**(d) Visibility**: PreToolUse's block-decision `reason` field naturally surfaces in the agent's transcript as a tool-use error, making the substitution visible to both the agent and the user. No additional surfacing needed.

## Implementation path

Filed as follow-up work-item li-hookimpl. Two-step landing: (1) propose-change → revise to add a §'Plugin-shipped hooks' to contracts.md declaring the hook surface and the specific Write-to-memory PreToolUse hook; (2) ship `.claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` + `.claude-plugin/hooks/block-auto-memory.sh`. Belt-and-suspenders complement to the sibling-repo (livespec-impl-plaintext) capture-memo SKILL.md discoverability fix.
reason: Research complete; all four open questions answered. Hook design: PreToolUse + Write matcher + `**/memory/*.md` filter + livespec-jsonc scoping check via `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` env var. Implementation depends on a prior spec change (plugin currently ships no hooks); follow-up impl work-item li-hookimpl filed.
title: Investigate Claude Code hook to redirect agent local auto-memory writes through capture-memo

description:
## Implementation follow-up to li-zmlkrl

Research completed in li-zmlkrl close-out. Two-step landing:

### Step 1 — spec change (propose-change → revise)

Add to `contracts.md` a new §"Plugin-shipped hooks" (or equivalent) declaring that `livespec` MAY ship Claude Code hooks via `.claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json`, and specifically ships a `PreToolUse` hook on the `Write` tool with matcher pattern `Write(**/memory/*.md)`. The hook redirects to `/livespec-impl-plaintext:capture-memo` when the active project carries a `.livespec.jsonc` declaring an impl-plugin with `memos_path`; otherwise the hook is a no-op pass-through. Document the hook-script contract (read `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`, check `.livespec.jsonc`, emit block-decision JSON on stdout for active projects, exit 0 otherwise).

### Step 2 — impl

Ship:
- `.claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` — declares the PreToolUse matcher.
- `.claude-plugin/hooks/block-auto-memory.sh` — the wrapper script.
- Unit-test the script via `tests/hooks/` (mock `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` + tmp_path).
- Wire into `just check`.

## Reference design

Plugin hooks fire automatically when installed. Response shape for blocking:

```json
{
  "decision": "block",
  "reason": "This project uses livespec. Use /livespec-impl-plaintext:capture-memo to file memos in the durable store.",
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
    "permissionDecision": "deny"
  }
}
```
title: Implement plugin-shipped PreToolUse hook redirecting auto-memory writes to capture-memo

description:
# Enforcement mechanisms for agent-collaboration discipline

Six items emerged from a session-end reflection on three observed failure modes during PR #42's bootstrap (bd auto-commit on master, vague /schedule gesturing, audit table living only in chat transcript). Items 1-4 are spec content (non-functional requirements). Items 5-6 are code/config that realize the spec rules.

## Status after PR #233 (2026-05-25)

Items 1-3 landed in PR #233 as a new `### Agent collaboration discipline` H3 in `non-functional-requirements.md`:
- Item 1 (destructive-default CLI wrapping) ✅
- Item 2 (verify before referring) ✅
- Item 3 (completion includes persistence and workspace cleanup) ✅

Items 4-6 spin out to li-1f5-rest:
- Item 4 (beads state-change consent boundary) — beads superseded by livespec-impl-plaintext; needs reframing for the JSONL store. Separate propose-change cycle.
- Item 5 (`dev-tooling/checks/no_direct_destructive_cli.py`) — realizes item 1; will surface as a spec→impl gap via `capture-impl-gaps` now that the rule is in NFR.
- Item 6 (Claude Code Stop hook for plan-artifact detection) — realizes item 3; will surface as a spec→impl gap.
reason: Items 1-3 (the spec-rule portion this work-item was filed to land) merged in PR #233 — added §'Agent collaboration discipline' to NFR with destructive-CLI-wrapping, verify-before-referring, and completion-includes-cleanup rules. Items 4-6 (item 4 needing reframing for the JSONL store post-beads-deprecation; items 5-6 gated on spec landing) spun out to li-1f5-rest for follow-up tracking. The main goal of li-1f5 is met.
title: Enforcement mechanisms for agent-collaboration discipline (4 spec rules + 2 code/config items)

description:
## Spin-out from li-1f5 (items 4-6)

Items 1-3 of li-1f5 landed in PR #233 (NFR §'Agent collaboration discipline'). Three remaining items tracked here.

### Item 4 — State-change consent boundary (reframed)

Original li-1f5 item 4 was scoped to beads. Beads has been superseded by livespec-impl-plaintext (JSONL stores at `work-items.jsonl` + `memos.jsonl`). Needs reframing for the new substrate, then propose-change → revise.

Draft principle: state-changing writes to the impl-plugin's persistent stores (work-items.jsonl, memos.jsonl, etc.) on the user's behalf MUST be per-operation user-consented unless the user has explicitly waived per-operation consent for the current operation class (e.g., 'auto-dispose all pending memos as <X>'). Batch-filing / auto-claiming / auto-closing without explicit per-operation OR waiver consent erodes the consent boundary that should govern every persistent artifact.

### Item 5 — `dev-tooling/checks/no_direct_destructive_cli.py` (gap-tied)

Realizes NFR §'Destructive-default CLI wrapping' (li-1f5 item 1, landed in PR #233). A dev-tooling check that greps `dev-tooling/`, `.claude-plugin/`, and `.claude/plugins/` for direct invocations of known-destructive-default CLIs (`bd init`, `git push --force`, `git reset --hard`, `gh repo delete`, etc.) outside an explicit allowlist. Fails CI on violation. Should surface as a spec→impl gap via `capture-impl-gaps` now that the rule is in NFR.

### Item 6 — Claude Code Stop hook for plan-artifact detection (gap-tied)

Realizes NFR §'Completion includes persistence and workspace cleanup' (li-1f5 item 3, landed in PR #233). A `.claude/settings.json` Stop hook that scans the agent's last turn for substantial planning artifacts (markdown tables above a row threshold, lists above an item threshold, heading-decorated planning content) and surfaces a warning when no recent tool call persisted equivalent content. The hook NEVER auto-files; it only surfaces the missed persistence. Could extend to surface workspace-cleanup gaps too (detect 'done' claims on non-master branches with merged history).
title: Agent collaboration discipline follow-ups: state-change consent (reframed) + items 5-6 realization

description:
## Why this issue exists

The `bcp14_keyword_wellformedness` static check at
`.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/bcp14_keyword_wellformedness.py`
applies a naive `\b(Must|Shall|Should|May)\b` regex against every
top-level `.md` file under `<spec_root>/`. Any occurrence of these
words at the start of a sentence — including unambiguously
descriptive English prose like `depends_on — array of id strings.
May be empty.` — flags as a malformed BCP 14 normative keyword.

The check's own docstring (lines 6-8) already acknowledges that
"sentence-level case-inconsistency detection moves to the LLM-driven
phase" — but the static rule still fires on descriptive
sentence-initial occurrences that have no realistic way to
distinguish from a botched normative usage without sentence-level
context.

## Reproduction

Documented at `livespec-impl-plaintext/SPECIFICATION/contracts.md:281`:

    - depends_on — array of id strings. May be empty.

Running `/livespec:doctor` against that spec produces a `fail`
Finding:

    contracts.md:281 mixed-case BCP 14 keyword 'May' (use uppercase
    'MAY' for normative usage)

The sentence is pure descriptive English ("the array can be empty")
— no normative force. Forcing the word to `MAY` would be wrong
(it's not granting permission to an implementer; it's describing
the data shape's allowable state).

## What needs to be done

Choose one of (a) or (b):

(a) **Defer `May` entirely to the LLM-driven phase.** Narrow the
    static rule to keywords vanishingly rare as descriptive English
    (most plausibly just `Shall`). Document the rationale in the
    check's docstring. Update the static check's tests to remove
    `May`/`Must`/`Should` coverage and add the cases to the
    LLM-driven objective-checks prompt.

(b) **Add sentence-level context heuristics to the static rule.**
    Only flag when the keyword is followed by an implementer-directed
    verb phrase (`be set`, `contain`, `support`, `implement`, etc.).
    Risk: heuristic complexity + false-negative class shift (real
    normative misuse outside the heuristic's pattern silently
    passes).

(a) is the simpler path and matches the check's own docstring
intent. (b) preserves more static coverage at higher implementation
cost.

## Acceptance criteria

- `/livespec:doctor` against `livespec-impl-plaintext` does NOT
  flag the descriptive "May be empty" prose at
  `contracts.md:281` (or equivalent descriptive uses anywhere
  in a spec tree).
- The static rule's coverage of legitimately-mixed-case BCP 14
  normative usage (e.g., "Implementations Must support …")
  either remains intact OR is explicitly documented as deferred
  to the LLM-driven phase.
- New test cases in
  `tests/livespec/doctor/static/test_bcp14_keyword_wellformedness.py`
  exercise both the descriptive-prose pass case AND any
  retained static-rule fail cases.

## Notes

First of three doctor bugs discovered during the 2026-05-24
cross-repo coordination automation surface investigation. Sibling
issues:

- `version_directories_complete` surfacing missing `proposed_changes/`
  as uncaught OSError (separate work-item).
- Bare invocation of `bin/doctor_static.py` requiring `uv run`
  because `livespec_runtime` is not vendored (separate work-item).
reason: PR #241 (0c90289, 66af9fa) landed 2026-05-25 on master. Narrowed bcp14 static rule to Shall only — Must/Should/May moved to LLM-driven phase per the bug's recommended path (a). Descriptive 'May be empty.' prose in impl-plaintext contracts.md no longer trips the static check.
title: Doctor bcp14_keyword_wellformedness flags descriptive sentence-initial 'May' as malformed BCP 14

description:
## Why this issue exists

The `version_directories_complete` static check at
`.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/version_directories_complete.py:145-150`
is explicit (per its own docstring at lines 124, 180-181) that the
fail arm for missing `proposed_changes/` "lands as
`IOFailure(PreconditionError)` via `fs.list_dir`" — the check
intentionally lets the missing-dir `OSError` bubble through the
framework's generic "check process error" wrapper.

The result: when a `<spec_root>/history/vNNN/` directory is missing
its `proposed_changes/` subdir, the user sees the cryptic:

    check process error: fs.list_dir: [Errno 2] No such file or
    directory: '.../history/vNNN/proposed_changes'

rather than a clean fail Finding like:

    history/vNNN/ is missing required subdir proposed_changes/

This is a UX bug, not a correctness bug — the failure IS detected,
but the surfacing obscures the real cause behind framework-error
noise. The user cannot tell at a glance whether the check broke
or the spec is broken.

## Reproduction

Documented during the 2026-05-24 investigation against the
`livespec-impl-plaintext` repo. Its `SPECIFICATION/history/v001/`
was missing the canonical `proposed_changes/` subdir. Running
`/livespec:doctor` surfaced the cryptic OSError rather than a
meaningful finding. The underlying spec-tree state has presumably
been corrected since (or is corrected as part of a future seed-bug
fix per `li-2qjqen`/PR #227); the doctor UX bug remains.

To reproduce synthetically: create any `history/vNNN/` containing
the template-required spec files but NO `proposed_changes/` subdir,
then run `/livespec:doctor` and observe the surfacing.

## What needs to be done

Update `version_directories_complete.py`'s `run` function (or
`_verify_version_dirs` helper) to catch the missing-dir case
explicitly BEFORE the `fs.list_dir` call would fail, and
synthesize a clean fail Finding:

    # rough sketch — adapt to the existing IOResult patterns:
    proposed_changes_path = version_path / "proposed_changes"
    if not proposed_changes_path.is_dir():
        accumulator = IOSuccess(_missing_proposed_changes_finding(
            ctx=ctx, version_path=version_path,
        ))
        continue
    # else fall through to existing list_dir probe

A new helper `_missing_proposed_changes_finding` constructs a
fail-Finding with `message` of the shape:

    history/vNNN/ is missing required subdir proposed_changes/

and `path` set to the offending v-dir for downstream
file-location surfacing.

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor against a spec tree with a `vNNN/` missing its
  `proposed_changes/` subdir surfaces a fail-Finding with a
  human-readable message naming the offending v-dir and the
  missing subdir.
- The "check process error" framework path is no longer triggered
  for this case (verifiable by absence of the `fs.list_dir: [Errno 2]`
  text in the finding message).
- New test case in
  `tests/livespec/doctor/static/test_version_directories_complete.py`
  exercises the missing-subdir branch with the expected message
  shape.
- Coverage gate (`fail_under = 100`) remains green.

## Notes

Second of three doctor bugs discovered during the 2026-05-24
investigation. Sibling issues:

- `bcp14_keyword_wellformedness` flagging descriptive "May"
  (separate work-item).
- Bare invocation of `bin/doctor_static.py` requiring `uv run`
  because `livespec_runtime` is not vendored (separate work-item).
reason: PR #242 (952a601) landed 2026-05-25 on master. _verify_version_dirs now branches on Path.is_dir() before fs.list_dir and synthesizes a clean fail-Finding ('history/vNNN/ is missing required subdir proposed_changes/') instead of letting OSError surface as a 'check process error' wrapper.
title: Doctor version_directories_complete surfaces missing proposed_changes/ subdir as uncaught OSError

description:
## Why this issue exists

The doctor static-phase wrapper at
`.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/doctor_static.py` imports
`livespec_runtime` transitively via several static checks:

- `scripts/livespec/doctor/static/depends_on_ref_wellformedness.py:40`:
  `from livespec_runtime.cross_repo.types import CrossRepoManifest`
- Plus 5 other static-check modules (`no_stale_gap_tied`,
  `no_orphan_dependency`, `no_stalled_epic`, etc.) and
  `livespec/parse/cross_repo.py`.

The plugin bundle's `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/` ships:

- `fastjsonschema`
- `jsoncomment`
- `returns`
- `structlog`
- `typing_extensions`

but does NOT ship `livespec_runtime`. The wrapper's
`_bootstrap.py` only prepends `scripts/` and `scripts/_vendor/`
to `sys.path`.

Result: bare invocation fails immediately with:

    ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'livespec_runtime'

Recovery: invoke via `uv run python bin/doctor_static.py …` from
a project venv that has `livespec-runtime` installed (the case
for every impl-plugin per its `pyproject.toml` git pin). This
works in practice for the typical use case BUT:

1. The `seed/SKILL.md` Step 1 and `doctor/SKILL.md` Step 1 prose
   says simply "Run `bin/doctor_static.py` via Bash" with no
   mention of `uv run` or the `livespec_runtime` precondition.
   A fresh agent following the prose verbatim crashes.

2. Bare invocation against a non-Python project (e.g., livespec
   doctor against a Markdown-only docs repo) cannot work at all
   under the current setup, even though doctor's spec-validation
   work has no Python-project prerequisite of its own.

3. `constraints.md` §"End-user runtime dependencies" promises
   `python3 >= 3.10` as the SOLE runtime dependency the skill
   imposes on end-user machines. The current wrapper silently
   violates that contract.

## What needs to be done

Two viable paths, identified during the 2026-05-24 research
session that ultimately landed the cross-repo coordination
automation surface (a strategic side-channel that mitigates
the operational toil objection to path (a)).

(a) **Vendor `livespec_runtime`** into
    `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/livespec_runtime/` per the
    existing 5-lib vendoring discipline. Re-vendoring on every
    `livespec-runtime` release is now automated by the
    `livespec-dev-tooling` `reusable-bump-pin-from-dispatch.yml`
    workflow (it detects `.vendor.jsonc` entries and invokes the
    consumer's `just vendor-update <lib>` recipe on dispatch).
    Spec-compliant per `constraints.md` §"Vendored-library
    discipline".

(b) **Document the `uv run` requirement** in `seed/SKILL.md` Step 1
    and `doctor/SKILL.md` Step 1, plus surface a clear precondition
    error from the wrapper when `livespec_runtime` is not
    importable (exit `127` with a directive to run via `uv run`
    or vendor the library). Cheaper but violates `constraints.md`
    §"End-user runtime dependencies" letter.

The 2026-05-24 research session recommended (a) as the canonical
Python ecosystem answer for self-bootstrapping tools (mirrors
pip's `_vendor/` pattern with ~18 packages). The cross-repo
coordination automation surface (now landed as
`livespec-dev-tooling` v0.2.0 via PR #6) provides the
version-sync mechanism that mitigates the toil objection.

## Acceptance criteria

For path (a) (recommended):

- `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/livespec_runtime/` exists with
  the full package tree at a pinned upstream tag (matching the
  `livespec-runtime` release the consumer pyproject.toml pin
  declares).
- `.vendor.jsonc` carries a sixth entry for `livespec_runtime`
  with `name`/`upstream_url`/`upstream_ref`/`vendored_at` fields
  per the existing schema.
- `just vendor-update livespec_runtime` re-vendoring path works
  (recipe updated if needed).
- Bare invocation `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/doctor_static.py
  --project-root <path>` succeeds against any project without
  requiring `uv run` or a project venv.
- The bump-pin automation
  (`livespec-dev-tooling`'s `reusable-bump-pin-from-dispatch.yml`)
  recognizes the new `.vendor.jsonc` entry and triggers
  `just vendor-update livespec_runtime` when `livespec-runtime`
  releases a new tag — verifiable via a follow-up bump-pin PR
  cycle.

For path (b) (alternative):

- `seed/SKILL.md` and `doctor/SKILL.md` Step 1 explicitly
  prescribe `uv run python bin/<wrapper>.py …` invocation.
- Wrapper detects missing `livespec_runtime` at startup and
  exits `127` with a clear directive instructing the operator
  to either install `livespec-runtime` in the project venv or
  invoke from a different context.
- Documentation explicitly acknowledges the constraint
  relaxation (livespec_runtime as a precondition the consumer
  must satisfy).

description:

The invariant MUST:
- Read the enumerated required-workflow-file list from contracts.md §Shared content sync — copier template (the list defined by the propose-change Edit 1).
- Walk the active project root .github/workflows/ directory.
- Fire 'fail' for every required workflow file that is missing from the consumer .github/workflows/.

- Be paired with an enforcement-suite check in livespec-dev-tooling that catches drift between the contract list and the doctor invariant hard-coded list (per the propose-change Edit 2 final paragraph — TBD in a parallel propose-change against that sibling spec; for v1 the doctor invariant MAY hard-code the list and rely on PR review).

Acceptance: doctor invocation against a fresh impl-plugin repo (generated from the template after li-tmpwfs lands) passes; doctor invocation against a hand-modified repo with one workflow file deleted fires fail naming that file; doctor invocation against livespec-impl-plaintext at HEAD (before li-tmpwfs lands) fires fail naming the missing files.

Dependency on li-tmpwfs is not blocking: the invariant can be implemented and tested against synthetic fixture projects before the real templates land. But the invariant remains harmless until the templates exist, since no consumer would yet have the workflow files to be MISSING.

description:
PC #1 (worktree-discipline-mechanical-enforcement, landed in v080) introduces the §"Bare-flag bootstrap procedure" NFR section mandating every livespec-governed repo surface an idempotent bootstrap entry point. Implement that entry point as a `just bootstrap` target in the project `justfile` (at <project-root>/justfile).

Contract per NFR:
1. The target MUST write `git config core.bare true` against `<project-root>/.git/config` so that subsequent `git pull`/`git checkout`/`git merge` against the primary checkout refuse and force every edit through `git worktree add`.
2. The target MUST be idempotent: running again on an already-bare primary checkout MUST be a no-op (no error, no side-effect).
3. The target MUST be documented in `README.md` and `CLAUDE.md` as a first-touch step on fresh clones.
4. The bare-flag step MUST be uncoupled from any other clone-time setup (lefthook installation, dependency installation, mise tool resolution) — a partial failure in one of those steps MUST NOT leave the bare-flag unset.

Acceptance:
- A fresh clone followed by `just bootstrap` leaves `core.bare = true` set.
- A second `just bootstrap` invocation against the same clone exits 0 with no observable change.
- The doctor `primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` invariant (PC #1 follow-up #2 in this set) passes against the bootstrapped clone.

Related work-items: see the two doctor-static-check follow-ups in this same batch — bootstrap + bootstrap-verifying invariant land together to form the complete enforcement loop.
title: PC #1 follow-up: implement `just bootstrap` target for core.bare=true

description:
PC #1 (worktree-discipline-mechanical-enforcement, landed in v080) introduces the `primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` doctor cross-boundary invariant in `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md`. Implement the static check module.

Module path: `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/primary_checkout_bare_flag_set.py`

Shape: mirror the existing `no_stale_worktree.py` static-check module shape — pure SLUG constant, a `check(...)` function returning `Iterable[Finding]`, registered in the static registry (`livespec/doctor/static/__init__.py`).

Behavior per contracts.md:
- Read `<project-root>/.git/config` and parse the `[core] bare` entry.
- Fire `fail` when `bare` is absent or set to `false` (treat both equally; the contract explicitly says MUST NOT distinguish).
- Narration MUST direct the user to invoke the documented bootstrap step (per §"Bare-flag bootstrap procedure" in non-functional-requirements.md), including the literal `git config core.bare true` command verbatim.
- The check applies to the primary checkout only. Secondary worktrees (per `git worktree list`) MUST NOT be inspected; the standalone `.git` files inside worktrees are not relevant.

Unit tests (paired test under `tests/livespec/doctor/static/test_primary_checkout_bare_flag_set.py`):
(a) bare-flag set → `pass` finding.
(b) bare-flag absent → `fail` finding with corrective bootstrap command in narration.
(c) bare-flag set explicitly to `false` → `fail` (same as absent, per contract).
(d) bare-repo (primary) with one or more secondary worktrees → `pass` (invariant reads only the primary `.git/config`).

Add the new check_id to `tests/doctor/test_static_registry.py` (or equivalent) if a registry-completeness test exists. Update heading-coverage.json if H2 headings changed (they didn't for this PC; H3 only).

Related work-items: see the bootstrap-target follow-up and the `master-direct-uncommitted-spec-edits` follow-up in this same batch.
title: PC #1 follow-up: implement `primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` doctor static check

description:
PC #1 (worktree-discipline-mechanical-enforcement, landed in v080) introduces the `master-direct-uncommitted-spec-edits` doctor cross-boundary invariant in `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md`. Implement the static check module.

Module path: `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/master_direct_uncommitted_spec_edits.py`

Shape: mirror the existing `no_stale_worktree.py` static-check module shape.

Behavior per contracts.md:
- Enumerate every worktree (primary + secondary) via `git worktree list --porcelain`.
- Identify the subset whose HEAD points at the default branch (typically `master`).
- For each such worktree, invoke `git status --porcelain` scoped to `<spec-root>/`.
- Any non-empty output fires a `warn` finding (NOT `fail` — consistent with the v079 prose at non-functional-requirements.md:746 — "a `warn` finding"). The corrective-action narration MUST: (1) name the offending worktree path; (2) name the modified files under `<spec-root>/`; (3) direct the user to either commit-into-a-feature-branch (`git checkout -b <branch>` then commit) per the workflow discipline, OR `git checkout -- <files>` to discard unintentional edits.

Why `warn` not `fail`: the violating commit is not yet pushed; the warning gives the user time to recover. Committed-and-then-discovered violations are out of scope here (covered by the existing `out-of-band-edits` check).

Unit tests (paired under `tests/livespec/doctor/static/test_master_direct_uncommitted_spec_edits.py`):
(a) primary worktree HEAD on `master`, no spec-tree modifications → `pass`.
(b) primary worktree HEAD on `master`, one modified file under <spec-root>/ → `warn` finding naming the worktree path + file path.
(c) primary worktree HEAD on a feature branch, modifications under <spec-root>/ → `pass` (HEAD is not the default branch).
(d) secondary worktree HEAD on `master` (created via `git worktree add <path> master`), with spec-tree modifications → `warn`. This is the bypass case the `primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` invariant cannot physically prevent.
(e) multiple worktrees all on master, mixed modification state → one `warn` per non-empty-status worktree.

Register the new check in the static registry. Related work-items: bootstrap-target follow-up and `primary-checkout-bare-flag-set` follow-up in this same batch.
title: PC #1 follow-up: implement `master-direct-uncommitted-spec-edits` doctor static check

description:
Cross-repo tooling discipline: avoid `uv run` against scripts hosted in sibling repos when their lockfile matters.

Memo origin: mm-6biors.

**Observed side effect:** running `uv run python <sibling-repo-script>` from a project's working directory rebuilds that project's `uv.lock` as a side effect (uv treats the cwd's pyproject.toml + uv.lock as the project context, re-resolves deps, and writes uv.lock).

**Specific 2026-05-24 occurrence:** invoked `uv run python /home/ubuntu/.claude/plugins/cache/livespec/livespec/<sha>/scripts/bin/doctor_static.py --project-root /data/projects/livespec-dev-tooling` from the dev-tooling project root because the doctor wrapper imports `livespec_runtime` which is not in the bundle's `_vendor/` tree (underlying bug — see work-item li-tvi7lm). The `uv run` re-resolved dev-tooling's project deps and modified its `uv.lock`. Had to `git restore uv.lock` before committing spec-side work.

**Workarounds (in priority order):**
1. Invoke from the repo whose `pyproject.toml` IS the right context for the script (rare in cross-repo scenarios).
2. `git restore uv.lock` after invocation, before committing.
3. Use `uv run --no-sync` to skip the lock-update step (verify the flag behaves as expected for the specific invocation).

**Deeper fix:** downstream of work-item li-tvi7lm (vendor `livespec_runtime`). Once vendored, doctor's wrapper no longer needs `uv run` at all and this side-effect can't happen via this code path. Other `uv run`-via-cross-repo-cwd scenarios may persist; if they bite agents repeatedly, an agent-discipline rule ("always `git restore` build artifacts after cross-repo `uv run` invocations") may belong in `non-functional-requirements.md` §"Completion includes persistence and workspace cleanup".
title: Cross-repo tooling discipline: avoid `uv run` against sibling-repo scripts when their lockfile matters

description:
## Why this issue exists

`.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/revise.py` can exit non-zero with NO
stderr output at the default log level (`LIVESPEC_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING`).
Re-running the identical invocation with `LIVESPEC_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG`
surfaced the precondition error and the run succeeded cleanly.

The wrapper is either:

- (a) Logging the precondition failure at INFO or DEBUG level so
  WARNING suppresses it before it reaches stderr, OR
- (b) Swallowing a returns/`Result` `IOFailure` in the supervisor
  pipeline without flowing it through structlog at a level above
  WARNING.

Either path violates the exit-code-contract intent in
`SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Non-interactive execution":
> A script that requires a human-confirmation step MUST fail with
> an actionable message and exit code `3` (precondition failed) ...

"Actionable message" implies stderr emission at any default log
level. The current behavior produces a silent non-zero exit, which
is indistinguishable from a hung wrapper for an operator running
under default settings.

## Reproducer

Run `bin/revise.py` with any `decisions[]` payload that triggers
an exit-3 precondition fail (e.g., `proposal_topic` referencing a
non-existent proposal file, or referencing only a section name
inside a multi-proposal file rather than a file stem - see the
`feedback_revise_decisions_are_per_file` memory entry) at the
default log level. Wrapper exits 3 with no stderr output.

Re-run with `LIVESPEC_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG` and the error narration
surfaces.

## Acceptance criteria

- Bare invocation of `bin/revise.py` failing for any reason MUST
  surface an actionable, human-readable message to stderr at any
  log level (including the default `WARNING`).
- Silent non-zero exits from the wrapper are forbidden - the
  supervisor pattern-match path that maps `IOFailure(err)` to
  `sys.exit(err.exit_code)` MUST also emit a structured-error JSON
  line on stderr at `ERROR` (or higher) level so default
  WARNING-and-above filtering surfaces it.
- Audit the other wrappers under `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/` for
  the same swallowing pattern (likely the same supervisor shim is
  shared); fix uniformly.
- Add a regression test under `tests/bin/` that asserts every
  exit-3 path emits at least one non-empty stderr line at default
  log level.

## Origin

Discovered 2026-05-26 by the PR #286 modified-migration-PC revise
sub-agent during the second-attempt revise pass. The agent's
first invocation exited 3 with no diagnostic; only the DEBUG
re-run revealed the actual precondition violation. Workaround:
always invoke with `LIVESPEC_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG` for revise wrapper
debugging.

## Notes

Not blocking - workaround (DEBUG level) is well-known and
documented in the in-flight memo entries. Priority P3 (UX bug,
not a correctness bug). Surfaces during agent-driven revise
iteration; rarely hit by interactive human users who already
default to verbose logging during spec authoring sessions.
title: bin/revise.py exits non-zero silently at default LIVESPEC_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING

description: Close record for li-revslnt. See open record (2026-05-27T02:10:00+00:00) for full reproducer + acceptance criteria.
reason: PR #291 merged: added log.error(...) call to bin/revise.py supervisor _pattern_match_io_result Failure arm so every non-zero exit emits a structlog ERROR-level diagnostic to stderr at default LIVESPEC_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING. Paired test test_revise_main_emits_diagnostic_on_failure_before_exit pins the regression via structlog.testing.capture_logs.
title: bin/revise.py exits non-zero silently at default LIVESPEC_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING

description:
Cross-repo follow-up to li-9i8 (canonical livespec ci.yml fix in PR #162 + #163, merged 2026-05-23/24).

The canonical fix dropped `--depth=1 2>/dev/null` from the
`detect-py-changes` step's `git fetch origin "$BASE_REF"` call and
ensured `fetch-depth: 0` on the checkout step. The shallow fetch was
intermittently flaking with "no merge base" on the subsequent
`git diff origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD`.

li-9i8 closed the livespec ci.yml itself but never propagated the
fix into:
1. The copier template `templates/impl-plugin/.github/workflows/ci.yml.jinja`
2. The 3 sibling repos' generated `.github/workflows/ci.yml` files
   (livespec-impl-plaintext, livespec-dev-tooling, livespec-runtime)

All 4 propagation PRs landed 2026-05-27. Cross-repo invariant
(canonical livespec ci.yml → template → sibling generated ci.yml)
is restored.
reason:
All 4 cross-repo propagation PRs landed:
- thewoolleyman/livespec#294 (copier template)
- thewoolleyman/livespec-impl-plaintext#46
- thewoolleyman/livespec-dev-tooling#31
- thewoolleyman/livespec-runtime#13

Each PR: 1 file, +10/-1. Total diff across 4 PRs: 4 files, +40/-4.
All merged via auto-merge or direct (siblings lack branch
protection on master).
title: Cross-repo ci.yml fetch-depth propagation (post-li-9i8)

description:
The new `## Reference discipline` section in `SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` (landed in v079) references the check as `doctor-anchor-reference-resolution`, but the existing static-phase check is registered as `anchor-reference-resolution` (no `doctor-` prefix). The same section introduces two new check IDs that DO carry the `doctor-` prefix (`doctor-no-cross-spec-reference`, `doctor-no-spec-section-citation-in-code`), suggesting an intended family-wide rename.

Decision is architectural — pick a convention going forward:
- (a) Family-wide rename: every existing doctor-static check_id gets a `doctor-` prefix. Touches the static-check registry at `livespec/doctor/static/__init__.py`, every check's SLUG constant, every test, and every prose reference. Heavy.
- (b) Drop the prefix on the new check IDs and update the constraints.md citation to `anchor-reference-resolution`. Light.

This work-item captures the decision-need, not the fix.

Source: /livespec:revise LLM-driven post-step finding, v079 cycle (2026-05-26).
reason: Investigation found the work-item premise factually incorrect: the SLUG constant in `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/anchor_reference_resolution.py` has been `doctor-anchor-reference-resolution` since the file was first created (commit e5289e9). No `check_id` literal in the codebase uses the bare slug `anchor-reference-resolution` — zero matches for the quoted string. All doctor-static check modules use the `doctor-` prefix uniformly in their SLUG constants. The bare-slug references found in prose (SKILL.md, spec.md, module docstrings) are descriptive uses of the "JSON slug" form per the explicit three-form convention codified in `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/CLAUDE.md`: "JSON slug `out-of-band-edits` ↔ module filename `out_of_band_edits.py` ↔ check_id `doctor-out-of-band-edits`." Three forms, all intentionally in use; no reconciliation needed. The v079 LLM-driven post-step that filed this work-item conflated the conceptual "JSON slug" form (correctly used in prose) with the `check_id` form (correctly prefixed in all check_id literals). Closed without code changes; no PR.
title: Reconcile check-ID prefix: `doctor-anchor-reference-resolution` vs existing `anchor-reference-resolution`
